blocks:
Sūrat Ash-Shu‘arā’

Meccan, and it is 227 verses.

Sūrat Ash-Shu‘arā’

It is entirely Meccan, except for His saying: وَالشُّعَراءُ يَتَّبِعُهُمُ الْغاوُونَ and the verses until the end of the Sūrah, for they were revealed in Medina. The number of its verses is two hundred and twenty-seven verses. بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) طسم (1) Ṭā Sīn Mīm In al-Majma‘, narrated from ‘Alī (peace be upon him), from the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his progeny): When طسم was revealed, He said: The ط (Ṭā’) is Mount Sinai (طور سينا), the س (Sīn) is Alexandria (اسكندرية), and the م (Mīm) is Mecca (مكّة). And He said: The ط (Ṭā’) is the Tūbā Tree (شجرة طوبى), the س (Sīn) is the Lote Tree of the Utmost Boundary (سدرة المنتهى), and the م (Mīm) is Muḥammad al-Muṣṭafā (may Allah bless him and his progeny). And al-Qummī said: طسم is among the letters of the Greatest Name of Allah (اسم الله الأعظم). And in al-Ma‘ānī, narrated from al-Ṣādiq (peace be upon him): As for طسم, its meaning is: I am the Seeker (الطالب), the All-Hearing (السميع), the Originator (المبدئ), the Restorer (المعيد).

(٢) تِلْكَ آياتُ الْكِتابِ الْمُبِينِ. (2) These are the signs of the manifest Book. (٣) لَعَلَّكَ باخِعٌ نَفْسَكَ ** (killing yourself) أَلَّا يَكُونُوا مُؤْمِنِينَ. (3) You are perhaps going to kill yourself out of grief because they will not be faithful. (٤) إِنْ نَشَأْ نُنَزِّلْ عَلَيْهِمْ مِنَ السَّماءِ ** (a sign of indication compelling them to faith, and a calamity forcing them upon it) **فَظَلَّتْ أَعْناقُهُمْ لَها خاضِعِينَ ** (submissive). (4) If We wish, We will send down upon them a sign from the sky, before which their necks will remain humbled.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Indeed, the القائم (peace be upon him) will not rise until a caller (مناد) calls out from the sky, which the young woman in her private chamber (خدرها) hears, and which the people of the East and the West hear. And concerning this, this verse was revealed: إِنْ نَشَأْ نُنَزِّلْ (If We wish, We will send down...) the verse.

And in al-Qummi, narrated from him (peace be upon him) concerning this verse, he said: "Their necks will be humbled," meaning بني أمية (Banu Umayyah), and it is the cry (الصّيحة) from the sky in the name of the Master of the Command (صاحب الأمر) (peace be upon him).

And in Irshad al-Mufid, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) concerning this verse, he said: "Allah will do that to them." He was asked: "Who are they?" He replied: "بنو أمية (Banu Umayyah) and their followers (شيعتهم)." He was asked: "And what is the sign (الآية)?" He replied: "The stagnation (ركود) of the sun between the sun's zenith (زوال الشمس) and the time of العصر (afternoon prayer), and the emergence of a chest and a face in the eye of the sun, known by his lineage (حسبه) and descent (نسبه). That will be in the time of السّفياني, and at that time will be his destruction (بواره) and the destruction of his people."

And in al-Ikmal, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him) in a narration describing the القائم (peace be upon him), he said: "And He is the one whom a caller (مناد) will call out for from the sky, which all the people of the earth will hear, calling them to Him. He will say: 'Behold! Indeed, the Proof of Allah (حجّة الله) has appeared near the House of Allah (بيت الله), so follow Him, for the truth is with Him.' And concerning Him is the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: إِنْ نَشَأْ نُنَزِّلْ عَلَيْهِمْ (If We wish, We will send down upon them...) the verse.

(٥) وَما يَأْتِيهِمْ مِنْ ذِكْرٍ مِنَ الرَّحْمنِ ** (And there does not come to them any reminder from the All-beneficent) (by His revelation to His Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله) مُحْدَثٍ ** (newly arrived) (meaning: its revelation is renewed) إِلَّا كانُوا عَنْهُ مُعْرِضِينَ ** (but they turn away from it) (except that they renew their turning away and their persistence (إصراراً) upon what they were previously upon).

(٦) فَقَدْ كَذَّبُوا ** (So they have denied) Meaning, they denied the Reminder (الذّكر) after their aversion, and they persisted deeply in denying it such that it led them to mockery (الاستهزاء). فَسَيَأْتِيهِمْأَنْباءُ ما كانُوا بِهِ يَسْتَهْزِؤُنَ ** (Soon there will come to them the news of what they have been ridiculing) concerning whether it was true or false, and whether it was worthy of being believed and its value magnified, or (worthy of) being denied, so its matter is treated lightly.

(٧) أَوَلَمْ يَرَوْا إِلَى الْأَرْضِ ** (7) Have they not regarded the earth, Meaning: Have they not looked at its wonders (عجائبها)? كَمْ أَنْبَتْنا فِيها مِنْ كُلِّ زَوْجٍ ** (how many We have grown in it of every splendid pair?) Meaning: kind (صنف). كَرِيمٍ ** (splendid?) Meaning: praiseworthy (محمود), abundant in benefit (كثير المنفعة). (٨) إِنَّ فِي ذلِكَ لَآيَةً ** (8) There is indeed a sign in that, Meaning: [A sign] that the One Who causes it to grow (منبتها) is perfect in Power and Wisdom (تام القدرة والحكمة), and abundant in Grace and Mercy (سابغ النعمة والرحمة). وَما كانَ أَكْثَرُهُمْ مُؤْمِنِينَ. (but most of them have not been believers.) (٩) وَإِنَّ رَبَّكَ لَهُوَ الْعَزِيزُ ** (9) Indeed your Lord is the All-mighty, Meaning: the Dominant (الغالب), the One Capable of taking vengeance upon the disbelievers (القادر على الانتقام من الكفرة). الرَّحِيمُ (the All-merciful.) Meaning: since He granted them respite (امهلهم). (١٠) وَإِذْ نادى رَبُّكَ مُوسى أَنِ ائْتِ الْقَوْمَ الظَّالِمِينَ ** (10) When your Lord called Moses, [saying,] ‘Go to the wrongdoing people, Meaning: [Wrongdoing] through disbelief (بالكفر), enslaving the Children of Israel (الاستعباد بني إسرائيل), and slaughtering their children (وذبح أولادهم). (١١) قَوْمَ فِرْعَوْنَ ** (11) the people of Pharaoh. Perhaps the restriction to "the people" (القوم) is due to the knowledge that Pharaoh is the one most deserving of that [designation]. أَلا يَتَّقُونَ (Will they not be wary [of Allah]?) Meaning: [This is] an expression of astonishment (تعجيب) at their excessiveness in wrongdoing (افراطهم في الظلم) and their audacity (واجترائهم). (١٢) قالَ رَبِّ إِنِّي أَخافُ أَنْ يُكَذِّبُونِ (12) He said, ‘My Lord, I fear that they will impugn me, (١٣) وَيَضِيقُ صَدْرِي وَلا يَنْطَلِقُ لِسانِي فَأَرْسِلْ إِلى هارُونَ ** (13) and my breast will be straitened, and my tongue will not be fluent. So send [the commission] to Aaron. Meaning: so that my heart may be strengthened by him (ليقوى به قلبي) and he may take my place (وينوب منابي) if I am afflicted by a speech impediment (الحبسة في اللّسان). (١٤) وَلَهُمْ عَلَيَّ ذَنْبٌ ** (14) Moreover, they have a charge against me, Meaning: the consequence of a sin (تبعة ذنب), which is the killing of the Copt (قتل القبطي). He called it a sin (ذنباً) according to their claim (على زعمهم). فَأَخافُ أَنْ يَقْتُلُونِ (so I fear that they will kill me) Meaning: because of it (به) before the delivery of the message (أداء الرسالة). (١٥) قالَ كَلَّا فَاذْهَبا ** (15) He said, ‘Certainly not! Go forth Meaning: [This is] an answer to his two requests (الطّلبتين). Meaning: refrain, O Moses, from what you suppose (ارتدع يا موسى عمّا تظنّ). So go forth, you and the one whom you requested (والذي طلبته). بِآياتِنا إِنَّا مَعَكُمْ ** (with Our signs. We are with you,) Meaning: [We are] listening (مُسْتَمِعُونَ) to what transpires between the two of you and him (meaning Moses, Aaron, and Pharaoh). So reveal what is incumbent upon you (فأظهر كما عليه).

(١٦) فَأْتِيا فِرْعَوْنَ فَقُولا إِنَّا رَسُولُ رَبِّ الْعالَمِينَ ** (16) So go to Pharaoh and say, 'We are the apostles of the Lord of all the worlds,' The word الرسول (messenger) was used in the singular because it is a verbal noun (مصدر) used as an attribute, and it is shared between the sender (المرسل) and the message (الرسالة).

(١٧) أَنْ أَرْسِلْ مَعَنا بَنِي إِسْرائِيلَ ** (17) 'to let the Children of Israel go with us.' Let them go with us to الشام (the Levant).

(١٨) قالَ أي فرعون لموسى بعد ان أتياه فقالا له ذلك أَلَمْ نُرَبِّكَ فِينا في منازلنا وَلِيداً طفلاً وَلَبِثْتَ فِينا مِنْ عُمُرِكَ سِنِينَ. (18) He said, 'Did we not raise you among us as a child, and did you not stay among us for years of your life? That is, Pharaoh said to Moses after they both came to him and said that to him: أَلَمْ نُرَبِّكَ فِينا ** (Did we not raise you among us) in our dwellings (منازلنا) وَلِيداً ** (as a child) meaning, a baby (طفلاً), and you remained among us for years of your life.

(١٩) وَفَعَلْتَ فَعْلَتَكَ الَّتِي فَعَلْتَ يعني قتل القبطي وبّخه به مُعظماً إيّاه بعد ما عدّد عليه نعمته وَأَنْتَ مِنَ الْكافِرِينَ بنعمتي. (19) And you committed the deed that you committed, and you are one of the ungrateful.' Meaning the killing of the Copt (قتل القبطي). He rebuked him for it, magnifying it, after enumerating His favor upon him: وَأَنْتَ مِنَ الْكافِرِينَ ** (and you are one of the ungrateful) meaning, ungrateful for My favor (بنعمتي).

Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) that he said: When Allah sent Moses to Pharaoh, he came to his gate and sought permission to enter, but he did not grant him permission. So, he struck the gate with his staff, and the doors clashed open. Then he entered upon Pharaoh and informed him that "I am رَسُولُ رَبِّ الْعالَمِينَ ** (the apostle of the Lord of all the worlds)," and he asked him to send the Children of Israel with him. Pharaoh then said to him, as Allah recounted: أَلَمْ نُرَبِّكَ ** (Did we not raise you) up to His saying: وَفَعَلْتَ فَعْلَتَكَ الَّتِي فَعَلْتَ (And you committed the deed that you committed), meaning: you killed the man, وَأَنْتَ مِنَ الْكافِرِينَ (and you are one of the ungrateful), meaning: you were ungrateful for My favor (كفرت نعمتي).

(٢٠) قالَ فَعَلْتُها إِذاً وَأَنَا مِنَ الضَّالِّينَ ** (20) He said, 'I did it then, and I was one of the astray.' It was said: [meaning] among the ignorant ones (الْجَاهِلِينَ). And in al-Uyun, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him), that he was asked about that, despite the fact that the Prophets are infallible (مَعْصُومُونَ). So he said: He said, وَأَنَا مِنَ الضَّالِّينَ (and I was one of the astray), meaning: concerning the path (عَنِ الطَّرِيقِ), due to my arrival in one of your cities. I say: Perhaps the intended meaning is that he used tawriyah (وَرَّى) with Pharaoh, and he intended being lost concerning the path (الضَّلَالُ عَنِ الطَّرِيقِ), but Pharaoh only understood from it ignorance (الْجَهْلَ) and being lost concerning the truth (الضَّلَالُ عَنِ الْحَقِّ). For being lost concerning the path does not serve as an excuse for the killing.

(٢١) فَفَرَرْتُ مِنْكُمْ لَمَّا خِفْتُكُمْ فَوَهَبَ لِي رَبِّي حُكْماً ** (wisdom) وَجَعَلَنِي مِنَ الْمُرْسَلِينَ. (21) So I fled from you when I feared you. Then my Lord granted me a judgement and made me one of the apostles. (٢٢) وَتِلْكَ نِعْمَةٌ تَمُنُّها عَلَيَّ أَنْ عَبَّدْتَ بَنِي إِسْرائِيلَ ** Meaning, and تِلْكَ ** (that) upbringing (التربية) نِعْمَةٌ تَمُنُّها عَلَيَ ** (is a favor you flaunt to me) outwardly (ظاهراً) by means of it, while in reality, it is your enslavement (تعبيدك) of the Children of Israel and your intention (وقصدهم) to slaughter their sons, for that is the reason (السبب) for my falling into your hands (وقوعي إليك) and my being raised (حصولي في تربيتك) under your care. And it is possible to estimate the interrogative particle (همزة الإنكار) [implying denial], meaning: Is تِلْكَ نِعْمَةٌ تَمُنُّها عَلَيَ ** (that a favor you flaunt to me), which is أَنْ عَبَّدْتَ.** (that you enslaved them)?

(٢٣) قالَ فِرْعَوْنُ وَما رَبُّ الْعالَمِينَ ** (23) Pharaoh said, 'And what is the Lord of all the worlds?' When he (Pharaoh) heard the response to that by which he had been challenged, and saw that he did not desist (لم يرعوِ) because of it, he began to object to his claim. So, he started by inquiring about the reality (حقيقة) of the Sender (المرسل). (٢٤) قالَ رَبُّ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ وَما بَيْنَهُمَا ** (24) He said, 'The Lord of the heavens and the earth and whatever is between them.' He (Moses) introduced Him (Allah) by His most manifest attributes (خواصه) and effects (آثاره). In al-Kafi, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), in the sermon (خطبة) of the comprehensive principles of Monotheism (جوامع التّوحيد), he said: "He is the One about Whom the Prophets asked, yet they did not describe Him by a limit (بحدّ) nor by a part (ببعض). Rather, they described Him by His actions (بفعاله) and pointed to Him by His signs (بآياته)." **إِنْ كُنْتُمْ مُوقِنِينَ ** (if you are certain) you would know that.

قالَ لِمَنْ حَوْلَهُ أَلا تَسْتَمِعُونَ ** (٢٥) (He said to those around him, 'Do you not listen?') His answer [came after] I asked Him about His reality (حقيقته), yet He mentions His actions (أفعاله).

In the previous narration, Al-Qummi said: He only asked him about the modality (كيفيّة) of Allah. Then Moses said: رَبُّ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ وَما بَيْنَهُمَا إِنْ كُنْتُمْ مُوقِنِينَ (٢٤) (The Lord of the heavens and the earth and whatever is between them, should you be certain.) Then Pharaoh said, marveling to his companions: أَلا تَسْتَمِعُونَ ** (Do you not listen?) I ask him about the modality (الكيفيّة), but he answers me concerning the truth (الحقّ).

أقولُ : Meaning, concerning the establishment (الثبوت).

(٢٦) قالَ رَبُّكُمْ وَرَبُّ آبائِكُمُ الْأَوَّلِينَ ** (26) He said, 'Your Lord and the Lord of your forefathers.' He shifted to that which there is no doubt concerning its need for a Wise Fashioner (مصوّر حكيم) and an All-Knowing Creator (خالق عليم), and which is closer to the observer (الناظر) and clearer to the contemplator (المتأمّل). (٢٧) قالَ إِنَّ رَسُولَكُمُ الَّذِي أُرْسِلَ إِلَيْكُمْ لَمَجْنُونٌ ** (27) He said, 'Indeed, your apostle who has been sent to you is crazy!' I ask him about one thing, and he answers me about another. And he called him an apostle (رسولاً) mockingly (على السّخرية). (٢٨) قالَ رَبُّ الْمَشْرِقِ وَالْمَغْرِبِ وَما بَيْنَهُما ** (28) He said, 'The Lord of the east and the west and whatever is between them,' You witness every day that He brings the sun from the east and takes it to the west in a beneficial manner by which the affairs of creation (أمور الخلق) are regulated. إِنْ كُنْتُمْ تَعْقِلُونَ ** (28) 'if you are sensible.' If you possess intellect (عقل), you would know that you have no answer beyond that. He was gentle with them first, then when He saw the severity of their obstinacy (شدّة شكيمتهم), He dealt harshly with them and countered them with the like of their own saying.

(٢٩) قالَ لَئِنِ اتَّخَذْتَ إِلهَاً غَيْرِي لَأَجْعَلَنَّكَ مِنَ الْمَسْجُونِينَ ** (29) He said, 'If you take any god other than Me, I will surely make you one of the imprisoned.' He shifted to threatening during the argumentation after being refuted (الانقطاع), and this is the habit (ديدن) of the obstinate one who has been defeated in argument (المحجوج). (٣٠) قالَ أَوَلَوْ جِئْتُكَ بِشَيْءٍ مُبِينٍ ** (30) He said, 'What, even if I bring you a manifest sign?' Meaning: Would you do that even if I brought you a manifest thing (بِشَيْءٍ مُبِينٍ) proving the truth of my claim? He means the miracle (المعجزة), for it combines the indication of the existence and wisdom of the Creator (الصانع) and the indication of the truthfulness of the claimant to prophethood. (٣١) قالَ فَأْتِ بِهِ إِنْ كُنْتَ مِنَ الصَّادِقِينَ (31) He said, 'Then bring it, if you are being truthful.' (٣٢) فَأَلْقى عَصاهُ فَإِذا هِيَ ثُعْبانٌ مُبِينٌ ** (32) So he cast down his staff, and behold, it was a manifest serpent. Manifest in its serpent-nature (الثعبانية). In al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): So it swallowed the portico (الايوان) with its jaws (بلحييها). Then he (Pharaoh) called out, "O Musa, grant me respite until tomorrow." Then what happened to him happened. (٣٣) وَنَزَعَ يَدَهُ فَإِذا هِيَ بَيْضاءُ لِلنَّاظِرِينَ ** (33) Then he drew out his hand, and behold, it was white to the onlookers. Al-Baqir (peace be upon him) or al-Majma' or others said (because if we refer back to Surat al-A'raf and Surat ash-Shu'ara' in al-Majma', we do not find the narration and the exegesis at the end of the two stories, and perhaps it is in another place, and Allah knows best): Its radiance (شعاعها) came between him and his face. And al-Qummi, in the previous narration, said: فَأَلْقى عَصاهُ فَإِذا هِيَ ثُعْبانٌ مُبِينٌ ** (So he cast down his staff, and behold, it was a manifest serpent.) Not one of Pharaoh's courtiers remained except that he fled, and such terror entered Pharaoh that he could not control himself. Pharaoh said, "O Musa, I implore you by Allah and by the suckling (بالرضاع), that you restrain it from me." Then نَزَعَ يَدَهُ فَإِذا هِيَ بَيْضاءُ لِلنَّاظِرِينَ ** (Then he drew out his hand, and behold, it was white to the onlookers.) When Musa took the staff, Pharaoh regained his composure (رجعت إلى فرعون نفسه) and intended to believe him. Haman stood up to him and said, "While you were a god who is worshipped, you have now become a follower who worships."

(٣٤) قالَ لِلْمَلَإِ حَوْلَهُ إِنَّ هذا لَساحِرٌ عَلِيمٌ ** (34) He said to the elite around him, 'This is indeed a skilled sorcerer, Excelling in the knowledge of sorcery. (٣٥) يُرِيدُ أَنْ يُخْرِجَكُمْ مِنْ أَرْضِكُمْ بِسِحْرِهِ فَما ذا تَأْمُرُونَ ** (35) who seeks to expel you from your land by his sorcery. So what do you bid?' The authority (سلطان) of the miracle overwhelmed him until it lowered him from the claim of Lordship (الربوبيّة) to consulting the people and seeking their counsel (ائتمارهم). (٣٦) قالُوا أَرْجِهْ وَأَخاهُ ** (36) They said, 'Put him and his brother off Delay their matter. وَابْعَثْ فِي الْمَدائِنِ حاشِرِينَ ** and send summoners to the cities, guards (شرطاً) who will gather the sorcerers. (٣٧) يَأْتُوكَ بِكُلِّ سَحَّارٍ عَلِيمٍ ** (37) who will bring you every skilled sorcerer.' Who are superior to him in this art (الفنّ). (٣٨) فَجُمِعَ السَّحَرَةُ لِمِيقاتِ يَوْمٍ مَعْلُومٍ ** (38) So the sorcerers were gathered for the tryst of a known day. For the time appointed for it, consisting of the hours of a specific day, which is the time of the forenoon (الضحى) on the Day of the Festival (يوم الزّينة), as preceded in Surat Taha. (٣٩) وَقِيلَ لِلنَّاسِ هَلْ أَنْتُمْ مُجْتَمِعُونَ ** (39) And it was said to the people, 'Will you gather? In this is a sense of finding their gathering slow, urging them to hasten to it. (٤٠) لَعَلَّنا نَتَّبِعُ السَّحَرَةَ إِنْ كانُوا هُمُ الْغالِبِينَ ** (40) Maybe we will follow the sorcerers if they are the victors.' Perhaps we will follow them in their religion if they are victorious. It is as if their original intention was not to follow Musa, not necessarily to follow the sorcerers, so they steered the speech in the manner of an allusion (الكناية). (٤١) فَلَمَّا جاءَ السَّحَرَةُ قالُوا لِفِرْعَوْنَ أَإِنَّ لَنا لَأَجْراً إِنْ كُنَّا نَحْنُ الْغالِبِينَ (41) When the sorcerers came, they said to Pharaoh, 'Shall we indeed have a reward if we are the victors?' (٤٢) قالَ نَعَمْ وَإِنَّكُمْ إِذاً لَمِنَ الْمُقَرَّبِينَ ** (42) He said, 'Yes, and then you will indeed be among the favored ones.' He committed to them the reward and closeness (القربة) to him, in addition to that, if they were victorious. (٤٣) قالَ لَهُمْ مُوسى أَلْقُوا ما أَنْتُمْ مُلْقُونَ ** (43) Musa said to them, 'Cast what you have to cast.' Meaning, after they had said to him: إِمَّا أَنْ تُلْقِيَ وَإِمَّا أَنْ نَكُونَ نَحْنُ الْمُلْقِينَ. (44) 'Either you cast, or we will be the casters.' (٤٤) فَأَلْقَوْا حِبالَهُمْ وَعِصِيَّهُمْ وَقالُوا بِعِزَّةِ فِرْعَوْنَ إِنَّا لَنَحْنُ الْغالِبُونَ ** (44) So they cast their ropes and their staffs, and said, 'By Pharaoh's might, we are indeed the victors!' They swore by his might (عزّته) that the victory belonged to them, due to the excess of their self-confidence and their bringing forth the utmost that could be brought forth in sorcery. This is one of the categories of the Age of Ignorance (الجاهلية), and in Islam, swearing is not valid except by Allah, the Mighty and Majestic (عزّ وجلّ).

(٤٥) فَأَلْقى مُوسى عَصاهُ فَإِذا هِيَ تَلْقَفُ ** (45) Then Moses cast down his staff, and behold, it swallowed up It swallows up (or: devours). And it was read with takhfif (lightening) ما يَأْفِكُونَ ** that which they turn away from its true nature (وجهه) through their deception (بتمويههم) and fabrication (وتزويرهم), whereby they make their ropes and staffs appear as if they are crawling serpents (حيّات تسعى). (٤٦) فَأُلْقِيَ السَّحَرَةُ ساجِدِينَ ** (46) Thereupon the magicians were cast down, prostrating. Due to their knowledge that the like of this cannot be achieved through magic (بالسحر). He only expressed the falling down (الخرور) using the term 'casting down' (بالإلقاء) so that it would correspond to what preceded it, and to indicate that when they saw what they saw, they could not control themselves (لم يتمالكوا أنفسهم). It was as if they were seized and thrown upon their faces, and that He, the Exalted, cast them down by means of the divine success (التّوفيق) He granted them.

(٤٧) قالُوا آمَنَّا بِرَبِّ الْعالَمِينَ (47) They said, 'We believe in the Lord of all the worlds,' (٤٨) رَبِّ مُوسى وَهارُونَ ** (48) the Lord of Moses and Aaron.' This is an apposition (ابدال) for clarification, to repel misconception, and to indicate that the cause (الموجب) for their faith is what He brought forth through the hands of the two of them. (٤٩) قالَ آمَنْتُمْ لَهُ ** He said, 'Did you believe in Him And it was read with two hamzas قَبْلَ أَنْ آذَنَ لَكُمْ إِنَّهُ لَكَبِيرُكُمُ الَّذِي عَلَّمَكُمُ السِّحْرَ ** before I gave you permission? He is indeed your chief who taught you magic. So he taught you something but not everything, and that is why he defeated you. Or, you made a pact with him—you conspired (تواطأتم) upon that. He intended by this to confuse (التّلبيس) his people so that they would not believe that they embraced faith based on insight (بصيرة) and the manifestation of truth (ظهور حقّ) فَلَسَوْفَ تَعْلَمُونَ ** Soon you will know. And the consequence (وبال) of what you have done لَأُقَطِّعَنَّ أَيْدِيَكُمْ وَأَرْجُلَكُمْ مِنْ خِلافٍ وَلَأُصَلِّبَنَّكُمْ أَجْمَعِينَ I will certainly cut off your hands and feet on opposite sides, and I will surely crucify you all.'

(٥٠) قالُوا لا ضَيْرَ ** (50) They said, 'There is no harm [to us]. There is no harm (ضرر) upon us in that. إِنَّا إِلى رَبِّنا مُنْقَلِبُونَ ** (50) Indeed, we are bound to return to our Lord. because of what You have threatened us with, for indeed, patience upon it is an eraser of sins (ممحاة للذنوب) and necessitates reward (الثواب) and proximity to Allah.

(٥١) إِنَّا نَطْمَعُ أَنْ يَغْفِرَ لَنا رَبُّنا خَطايانا أَنْ كُنَّا أَوَّلَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ** (51) Indeed, we are eager that our Lord will forgive us our iniquities, for we were the first of the believers.' from the people of the gathering (المشهد). And it was recited (وقرئ) as إن with the kasrah on the hamzah.

Al-Qummi, in the previous narration, said (peace be upon him): Pharaoh and Haman had learned sorcery (السّحر), and they only overcame the people through sorcery. Pharaoh claimed Lordship (الربوبيّة) through sorcery. When morning came, he sent **فِي الْمَدائِنِ حاشِرِينَ ** (79) into the cities summoners, all the cities of Egypt, and they gathered a thousand sorcerers. They chose one hundred from the thousand, and eighty from the hundred.

So the sorcerers said to Pharaoh: "You know that there is no one in the world more skilled in sorcery (أسحر) than us. If Moses overcomes us, what will we have with you?" He said: إِنَّكُمْ إِذاً لَمِنَ الْمُقَرَّبِينَ ** (42) 'In that case you will indeed be among the proximity-gainers [to me].' with me; I will share My kingdom (ملكي) with you. They said: "But if Moses overcomes us and nullifies our sorcery (سحرنا), we will know that what He has brought is not from sorcery (السّحر) nor from trickery (الحيلة). We will believe in Him and affirm Him." Pharaoh said: "If Moses overcomes you, I too will affirm Him with you. But gather your plot (كيدكم)," meaning your trickery (حيلتكم).

He said: Their appointed time was a day of celebration (عيد) for them. When the day rose high, Pharaoh gathered the creation (الخلق) and the sorcerers. He had a dome (قبّة) whose height in the sky was eighty cubits (ذراعاً). It had been clad in polished iron (الحديد) and steel (الفولاذ). When the sun fell upon it, no one could look at it due to the gleam of the iron and the glare of the sun. Pharaoh and Haman came and sat upon it, looking on. Moses approached, looking towards the sky. So the sorcerers said to Pharaoh: "We see a man looking towards the sky, and our sorcery (سحرنا) does not reach the sky." The sorcerers guaranteed [control over] those on the earth. So they said to Moses: إِمَّا أَنْ تُلْقِيَ وَإِمَّا أَنْ نَكُونَ نَحْنُ الْمُلْقِينَ قالَ لَهُمْ مُوسى أَلْقُوا ما أَنْتُمْ مُلْقُونَ فَأَلْقَوْا حِبالَهُمْ وَعِصِيَّهُمْ ** (43) 'Either you cast, or we will be the casters.' (44) He said to them, 'Cast whatever you have to cast.' So they cast their ropes and their staffs. They began to writhe like snakes. Then قالُوا بِعِزَّةِ فِرْعَوْنَ إِنَّا لَنَحْنُ الْغالِبُونَ فَأَوْجَسَ فِي نَفْسِهِ خِيفَةً مُوسى ** (44) They said, 'By Pharaoh's might, it is surely we who are the victors!' (45) Moses felt a fear within his heart. So He called out: لا تَخَفْ إِنَّكَ أَنْتَ الْأَعْلى وَأَلْقِ ما فِي يَمِينِكَ تَلْقَفْ ما صَنَعُوا إِنَّما صَنَعُوا كَيْدُ ساحِرٍ ** (68) 'Do not fear! It is surely you who will be superior. (69) Cast what is in your right hand: it will swallow what they have wrought. What they have wrought is only a sorcerer's trick. So Moses cast the staff, and it melted into the earth like lead (الرصاص). Then its head emerged, and it opened its mouth (فاها), placing its upper jaw (شدقها العليا) on the top of Pharaoh's dome (قبّة). Then it turned, lowered its lower lip (شفتها السفلى), and swallowed the sorcerers' staffs and ropes, overcoming all of them. And the people fled when they saw it, its magnitude (عظمها), and its terror (هولها), something the eye had never seen nor the describers (الواصفون) described the like of. So, ten thousand men, women, and children were killed in the stampede (الهزيمة) as people trampled one another. And it circled Pharaoh's dome. He said: Pharaoh and Haman soiled their clothes and their heads turned gray from the terror (الفزع). Moses passed by in the stampede along with the people. So Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, called out to Him: خُذْها وَلا تَخَفْ سَنُعِيدُها سِيرَتَهَا الْأُولى ** (21) 'Take it and do not fear. We shall restore it to its former state.' So Moses returned and wrapped his cloak (عبائه)—which was upon Him—around His hand. Then He put His hand into its mouth, and behold, it was a staff as it had been. And it was as Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said: فَأُلْقِيَ السَّحَرَةُ ساجِدِينَ (The sorcerers were cast down, prostrating,) when they saw that. قالُوا آمَنَّا بِرَبِّ الْعالَمِينَ رَبِّ مُوسى وَهارُونَ ** (46) They said, 'We believe in the Lord of the worlds, (47) the Lord of Moses and Aaron.' Pharaoh became intensely angry at that point and قالَ آمَنْتُمْ لَهُ قَبْلَ أَنْ آذَنَ لَكُمْ إِنَّهُ لَكَبِيرُكُمُ ** (71) said, 'Did you believe in Him before I gave you leave? He is indeed your chief meaning Moses, الَّذِي عَلَّمَكُمُ السِّحْرَ (who taught you sorcery.) (the rest of the Ayah). So they said to him, as Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, related: لا ضَيْرَ (There is no harm) (the two Ayahs). So Pharaoh imprisoned those who believed in Moses until Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, sent down upon them the flood (الطوفان), the locusts (الجراد), the lice (القمل), the frogs (الضفادع), and the blood (الدم). Then He released them.

(52) وَأَوْحَيْنا إِلى مُوسى أَنْ أَسْرِ بِعِبادِي ** (52) We revealed to Moses: 'Set out with My servants by night; It was said that this was after years he stayed among them, calling them to the truth (الحقّ) and manifesting the signs (الآيات) to them, but they only increased in insolence (عتوّاً) and corruption (فساداً). إِنَّكُمْ مُتَّبَعُونَ ** you will be pursued.' Pharaoh and his soldiers (جنوده) will pursue you.

(٥٣) فَأَرْسَلَ فِرْعَوْنُ ** حين اخبر بسراه فِي الْمَدائِنِ حاشِرِينَ ** العساكر ليتّبعوهم. (53) So Pharaoh sent (into the cities gatherers) (when he was informed of their night journey) (into the cities gatherers) (of) the armies, so that they might pursue them.

(٥٤) إِنَّ هؤُلاءِ لَشِرْذِمَةٌ قَلِيلُونَ ** على إرادة القول. (54) (Indeed, these are a small band)** (intending the saying).

القمّيّ عن الباقر عليه السلام : يقول عصبة قليلة. Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He says: a small group (عصبة قليلة).

(٥٥) وَإِنَّهُمْ لَنا لَغائِظُونَ ** لفاعلون ما يغيظنا. (55) (And indeed, they are enraging Us)** meaning: they are doing what angers us.

(٥٦) وَإِنَّا لَجَمِيعٌ حاذِرُونَ ** وانّا لجمع من عادتنا الحذر واستعمال الحزم في الأمور وقرء بحذف الالف. (56) (And indeed, we are all watchful) And indeed, we are a multitude whose custom is caution (الحذر) and the employment of firmness (الحزم) in affairs. And it was recited by omitting the alif.

القمّيّ في الحديث : السّابق : فخرج موسى ببني إسرائيل ليقطع بهم البحر وجمع فرعون أصحابه وَابْعَثْ فِي الْمَدائِنِ حاشِرِينَ ** وحشر الناس وقدم مقدّمته في ستّة مائة الف وركب هو في ألف ألف وخرج كما حكى الله. Al-Qummi, in the preceding narration: So Moses went out with the Children of Israel to cross the sea with them, and Pharaoh gathered his companions (saying): وَابْعَثْ فِي الْمَدائِنِ حاشِرِينَ ** (And send into the cities gatherers) And he gathered the people and sent his vanguard (مقدّمته) in six hundred thousand, and he himself rode with one million (ألف ألف), and he went out as Allah recounted.

(٥٧) فَأَخْرَجْناهُمْ مِنْ جَنَّاتٍ وَعُيُونٍ. (57) Thus We expelled them from gardens and springs, (٥٨) وَكُنُوزٍ وَمُقامٍ كَرِيمٍ ** (58) and treasures and a noble station. meaning: the beautiful dwellings (المنازل الحسنة) and the splendid assemblies (المجالس البهيّة). (٥٩) كَذلِكَ ** (59) So it was, and We made the Children of Israel their inheritors. Like that expulsion (الإخراج), وَأَوْرَثْناها بَنِي إِسْرائِيلَ (٦٠) فَأَتْبَعُوهُمْ مُشْرِقِينَ ** (60) So they pursued them at sunrise. entering at the time of the rising of the sun (شروق الشمس). (٦١) فَلَمَّا تَراءَا الْجَمْعانِ ** (61) When the two hosts sighted each other, the companions of Moses said, ‘We are surely overtaken!’ They drew near so that each one of them saw the other, قالَ أَصْحابُ مُوسى إِنَّا لَمُدْرَكُونَ ** meaning: surely overtaken (لملحقون). (٦٢) قالَ كَلَّا ** (62) He said, ‘No indeed! My Lord is with me; He will guide me.’ They will not overtake you, for Allah has promised you deliverance (الخلاص) from them. إِنَّ مَعِي رَبِّي ** with preservation (الحفظ) and assistance (النصرة), سَيَهْدِينِ ** to the path of salvation (طريق النجاة) from them. (٦٣) فَأَوْحَيْنا إِلى مُوسى أَنِ اضْرِبْ بِعَصاكَ الْبَحْرَ فَانْفَلَقَ ** (63) Then We revealed to Moses: ‘Strike the sea with your staff.’ Thereupon it parted, and each part was like a massive mountain. Meaning: he struck, so it parted, فَكانَ كُلُّ فِرْقٍ كَالطَّوْدِ الْعَظِيمِ like the lofty mountain (الجبل المنيف), firm in its place (الثابت في مقرّه), so they entered its paths (شعابها). (٦٤) وَأَزْلَفْنا ** (64) And We brought the others near to that place. And We brought near (وقرّبنا) ثَمَّ الْآخَرِينَ ** Pharaoh and his people (فرعون وقومه), until they entered their entrances (مداخلهم) following their tracks (على أثرهم). (٦٥) وَأَنْجَيْنا مُوسى وَمَنْ مَعَهُ أَجْمَعِينَ ** (65) And We delivered Moses and all those who were with him. by preserving the sea in that state (تلك الهيئة) until they crossed (عبروا). (٦٦) ثُمَّ أَغْرَقْنَا الْآخَرِينَ ** (66) Then We drowned the others. by its closing upon them (باطباقه عليهم). (٦٧) إِنَّ فِي ذلِكَ لَآيَةً ** (67) There is indeed a sign in that; but most of them have not been believers. And what a sign (وايّة آية)! وَما كانَ أَكْثَرُهُمْ مُؤْمِنِينَ ** And most of them did not pay attention to it (تنبّه عليها), since none of those who remained in Egypt (مصر) from the Copts (القبط) believed in it, and the Children of Israel (بنو إسرائيل), after they were saved, asked for a cow (بقرة) to worship and took the Calf (العجل), and said: We will never نُؤْمِنَ لَكَ حَتَّى نَرَى اللهَ جَهْرَةً.** (We will not believe you unless we see Allah manifestly.)

(٦٨) وَإِنَّ رَبَّكَ لَهُوَ الْعَزِيزُ الرَّحِيمُ (68) And indeed your Lord is the All-mighty, the All-merciful.

وَإِنَّ رَبَّكَ لَهُوَ الْعَزِيزُ (And indeed your Lord is the All-mighty) so that He may take vengeance upon His enemies الرَّحِيمُ (the All-merciful) towards His allies (أوليائه).

Al-Qummi in the previous narrative (الحديث السابق): When Moses (peace be upon him) drew near the sea, and Pharaoh drew near Moses, قالَ أَصْحابُ مُوسى إِنَّا لَمُدْرَكُونَ قالَ (The companions of Moses said, 'We are surely overtaken!') He said, Moses: "Nay, indeed my Lord is with me; He will guide me," meaning: He will save me (سينجيني). So Moses approached the sea and said to it: "Part!" The sea said: "You have become arrogant, O Moses, that I should part for you, when I have not disobeyed Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, for the blink of an eye, and yet the disobedient one (العاصي) was among you." So Moses said to it: "Then beware of disobeying, for you know that Adam was expelled from Paradise due to his disobedience, and Iblis was cursed only due to his disobedience." The sea said: "My Lord is Great, His command is obeyed, and it is not proper for anything to disobey Him." Then Joshua bin Nun (يوشع بن نون) stood up and said to Moses: "O Prophet of Allah, what has your Lord commanded you?" He said: "To cross the sea." So Joshua plunged his horse into the water. Then Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, revealed إِلى مُوسى أَنِ اضْرِبْ بِعَصاكَ الْبَحْرَ to Moses, 'Strike the sea with your staff.' So he struck it, and it parted, فَكانَ كُلُّ فِرْقٍ كَالطَّوْدِ الْعَظِيمِ (and every part was like a great mountain) meaning: like the great mountain (كالجبل العظيم). And He struck for them twelve paths in the sea, and every tribe (سبط) among them took a path. The water had risen, and the earth remained dry; the sun rose and dried it, just as Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, related: فَاضْرِبْ لَهُمْ طَرِيقاً فِي الْبَحْرِ يَبَساً لا تَخافُ دَرَكاً وَلا تَخْشى 'Strike for them a dry path in the sea. Do not fear being overtaken, nor be afraid.' And Moses (peace be upon him) and his companions entered the sea. His companions were twelve tribes (سبط). So Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, struck for them twelve paths in the sea, and every tribe took a path. The water had risen above their heads like mountains. The group that was with Moses in his path became anxious and said: "O Moses, where are our brothers?" He said to them: "They are with you in the sea." But they did not believe him. So Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, commanded the sea, and it became like windows (طاقات) so that they could look at one another and converse. Then Pharaoh and his soldiers approached. When he reached the sea, he said to his companions: "Do you not know that I am your Lord, the Most High? The sea has parted for me!" No one dared to enter the sea, and the horses refused due to the terror of the water. So Pharaoh advanced until he reached the seashore. His astrologer (منجّمه) said to him: "Do not enter the sea," and opposed him, but he did not accept his advice. He approached a stallion (فرس حصان), but the stallion refused to enter the water. Jibrāʾīl (جبرائيل) then turned towards him while riding a mare (ماذيانة), advanced ahead of him, and entered. The stallion descended toward the mare (الرّمكّة), sought her, and entered the sea. His companions plunged in behind him. When they had all entered, such that the last of his companions had entered and the last of Moses' companions had exited, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, commanded the winds, and they struck the sea against itself. The water began to fall upon them like mountains. Pharaoh then said: آمَنْتُ أَنَّهُ لا إِلَهَ إِلَّا الَّذِي آمَنَتْ بِهِ بَنُوا إِسْرائِيلَ وَأَنَا مِنَ الْمُسْلِمِينَ (I believe that there is no god except Him in Whom the Children of Israel believe, and I am one of the submitters.) Then Jibrāʾīl took a handful of mud (حماة) and stuffed it into his mouth, then said: آلْآنَ وَقَدْ عَصَيْتَ قَبْلُ وَكُنْتَ مِنَ الْمُفْسِدِينَ Now? While you disobeyed before and were one of the agents of corruption! Some of this story has passed in Surah Yunus, and another part in Surah Ta Ha.

And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), He said: Indeed, a group of those who believed in Moses said: "If only we went to Pharaoh's army and were among them, we could attain some of his worldly goods. Then, when what we hope for—the triumph of Moses—occurs, we will return to him." So they did this. When Moses and those with him set out fleeing from Pharaoh, they mounted their beasts and hastened their journey to catch up with Moses and his army so they could be with them. But Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, sent an angel who struck the faces of their beasts, turning them back toward Pharaoh's army. Thus, they were among those who drowned with Pharaoh.

(٦٩) وَاتْلُ عَلَيْهِمْ نَبَأَ إِبْراهِيمَ (69) And recite to them the account of Abraham, upon the polytheists of the Arabs. (٧٠) إِذْ قالَ لِأَبِيهِ وَقَوْمِهِ ما تَعْبُدُونَ ** (70) when he said to his father and his people, ‘What do you worship?’ He asked them in order to show them that what they worship does not deserve worship (العبادة). (٧١) قالُوا نَعْبُدُ أَصْناماً فَنَظَلُّ لَها عاكِفِينَ ** (71) They said, ‘We worship idols, and we remain devoted to them.’ They prolonged their answer by way of pretext (تحجّجاً) and boasting (افتخاراً). (٧٢) قالَ هَلْ يَسْمَعُونَكُمْ إِذْ تَدْعُونَ ** (72) He said, ‘Do they hear you when you call out [to them], They hear your supplication (دعاءكم). (٧٣) أَوْ يَنْفَعُونَكُمْ أَوْ يَضُرُّونَ ** (73) or do they benefit or harm you?’ due to your worship of them, or harm you, those who turn away from them. (٧٤) قالُوا بَلْ وَجَدْنا آباءَنا كَذلِكَ يَفْعَلُونَ ** (74) They said, ‘No, but we found our fathers doing that.’ They ignored his answer and resorted to imitation (التقليد). (٧٥) قالَ أَفَرَأَيْتُمْ ما كُنْتُمْ تَعْبُدُونَ. (75) He said, ‘Have you considered what you have been worshipping, (٧٦) أَنْتُمْ وَآباؤُكُمُ الْأَقْدَمُونَ. (76) you and your forefathers of yore? (٧٧) فَإِنَّهُمْ عَدُوٌّ لِي إِلَّا رَبَّ الْعالَمِينَ ** (77) They are my enemies, except the Lord of all the worlds, He means: they are enemies to you, but he depicted the matter as pertaining to himself by way of allusion (تعريضاً) to them, because this is more beneficial in sincere advice (النّصح) than explicit statement (التصريح), and beginning the advice with himself is more conducive to acceptance. إِلَّا رَبَّ الْعالَمِينَ ** (except the Lord of all the worlds,) This is an interrupted exception (استثناء منقطع) or a connected exception (متّصل) based on the understanding that the pronoun refers to every object of worship (معبود) they worshipped, and some of their forefathers worshipped Allah.

(٧٨) الَّذِي خَلَقَنِي فَهُوَ يَهْدِينِ ** (78) who created me, and He guides me, Because He guides every creature to that for which it was created, concerning the affairs of livelihood (المعاش) and the Hereafter (المعاد), just as He said: الَّذِي أَعْطى كُلَّ شَيْءٍ خَلْقَهُ ثُمَّ هَدى ** (Who gave everything its creation, then guided it.) [This is] a gradual guidance (هداية مدرجة) from the beginning of existence (الإيجاد) until the end of its appointed term (أجله).

(٧٩) وَالَّذِي هُوَ يُطْعِمُنِي وَيَسْقِينِ (79) and He who feeds me and gives me to drink, (٨٠) وَإِذا مَرِضْتُ فَهُوَ يَشْفِينِ ** The sickness was only not attributed to Him because his purpose was the enumeration of blessings (النِّعم), and because, in most cases (غالب الامر), it only occurs due to man's negligence (تفريط) regarding his foods (مطاعمه) and drinks (مشاربه), and regarding the commands (أوامر) and prohibitions (نواهيه) of Allah. Just as Allah, the Glorious, said: ما أَصابَكُمْ مِنْ مُصِيبَةٍ فَبِما كَسَبَتْ أَيْدِيكُمْ** (Whatever affliction visits you is because of what your hands have earned)

(٨١) وَالَّذِي يُمِيتُنِي ** (81) (and He who makes me die) He counted death among the blessings and attributed it to Allah because for the people of perfection (أهل الكمال), it is a means (وصلة) to attain the beloved things (المحابّ) before which the worldly life is deemed insignificant, and a deliverance from various kinds of trials (المحن) and afflictions (البليّة). ثُمَّ يُحْيِينِ ** (then He will revive me) in the Hereafter.

(٨٢) **وَالَّذِي أَطْمَعُ أَنْ يَغْفِرَ لِي خَطِيئَتِي يَوْمَ الدِّينِ ** (82) and who I hope will forgive me my faults on the Day of Retribution.’

He mentioned that out of self-abasement (هضماً لنفسه) and as an instruction to the Ummah that they should avoid acts of disobedience (المعاصي) and be vigilant, seeking that He forgive them what they neglect (ما يفرط منهم), and seeking forgiveness for what might rarely occur from him that is contrary to the preferable course (خلاف الأولى). And interpreting the sin (الخطيئة) as referring to his three statements: إِنِّي سَقِيمٌ بَلْ فَعَلَهُ كَبِيرُهُمْ ** and his saying 'She is my sister' has no basis (لا وجه له), because they are ambiguous statements (معاريض) and are not sins (خطايا).

(٨٣) رَبِّ هَبْ لِي حُكْماً ** (83) My Lord! Grant me judgement (Grant me) perfection (كمالاً) in knowledge (العلم) and action (العمل), by which I may prepare myself for the vicegerency (خلافة) of the Truth (الحقّ) and the leadership (رياسة) of creation (الخلق). وَأَلْحِقْنِي بِالصَّالِحِينَ ** and join me with the righteous. And grant me success (وفّقني) in achieving perfection (الكمال) in action (العمل), so that I may be included thereby in the ranks (عداد) of those who are perfect (الكاملين) in righteousness (الصلاح). (٨٤) وَاجْعَلْ لِي لِسانَ صِدْقٍ فِي الْآخِرِينَ ** (84) and appoint for me a truthful repute among the posterity. (Grant me) high standing (جاهاً) and good renown (حسن صيت) in this world (الدنيا) whose effect (اثره) remains until the Day of Judgment (يوم الدين). And for this reason, there is no nation (أمّة) except that they are lovers of him (محبّون له) and praisers of him (مثنون عليه).

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: The Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) said: A truthful repute (لِسانَ صِدْقٍ) for a man, which Allah places among the people, is better for him than wealth (المال) which he consumes and bequeaths. Or the intended meaning is: وَاجْعَلْ ** (And appoint) a truthful one (صادقاً) from my progeny (ذرّيتي) who renews the foundation (أصل) of my religion (ديني) and calls people to that which I used to call them to, and that is Muhammad (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) and Ali (عليه السلام) and the Imams (peace be upon them) from their progeny (ذرّيتهما). Al-Qummi said: He is the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him).

(٨٥) وَاجْعَلْنِي مِنْ وَرَثَةِ جَنَّةِ النَّعِيمِ ** (85) and make me one of the inheritors of the Garden of Bliss. in the Hereafter. And the meaning of inheritance (الوراثة) therein has already preceded in Sūrat al-Mu’minūn. (٨٦) وَاغْفِرْ لِأَبِي ** (86) and forgive my father, through guidance (الهداية) and granting success (التوفيق) to faith (الإيمان). إِنَّهُ كانَ مِنَ الضَّالِّينَ ** (86) indeed he was one of the astray. from the path of truth. And he only prayed for forgiveness for him when he promised him that he would believe, as Allah the Almighty said: وَما كانَ اسْتِغْفارُ إِبْراهِيمَ لِأَبِيهِ إِلَّا عَنْ مَوْعِدَةٍ وَعَدَها إِيَّاهُ.** (114) Abraham’s prayer for forgiveness for his father was only because of a promise He had made him.

(٨٧) وَلا تُخْزِنِي ** (87) and do not disgrace me Meaning: by reproaching me for what I neglected, where الخزي (disgrace) means humiliation (الهوان), or from الخزاية (shame) meaning modesty (الحياء). يَوْمَ يُبْعَثُونَ ** (on the day when they will be resurrected). The pronoun (الضّمير) refers to the servants (العباد) because they are known.

(٨٨) يَوْمَ لا يَنْفَعُ مالٌ وَلا بَنُونَ (88) the day when neither wealth nor children will avail,

(٨٩) إِلَّا مَنْ أَتَى اللهَ بِقَلْبٍ سَلِيمٍ ** (89) except him who comes to Allah with a sound heart. Meaning: neither of them (wealth nor children) will benefit anyone except the sincere one (مخلصاً) who possesses a sound heart (سليم القلب).

In al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), He said: "It is the heart that is safe (سلم) from the love of the world (الدّنيا)." And in al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): He was asked about this verse, and He said: "The sound heart (القلب السليم) is that which meets its Lord, and there is no one in it besides Him." He said: "And every heart in which there is polytheism (شرك) or doubt (شكّ) is worthless (ساقط). Indeed, they intended by asceticism (الزّهد) in the world (الدّنيا) that their hearts might be emptied for the Hereafter (الآخرة)."

And in Misbah al-Shari'ah, al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) said: "The possessor of sincere intention (النية الصادقة) is the possessor of the sound heart (القلب السليم), because the soundness (سلامة) of the heart from the anxieties (هواجس) of the mentioned things purifies the intention (النيّة) for Allah in all matters." Then He recited this verse.

(٩٠) وَأُزْلِفَتِ الْجَنَّةُ لِلْمُتَّقِينَ ** (90) And Paradise will be brought near for the Godwary, such that they see it from the standing place (الموقف), and they rejoice that they are the ones gathered to it. (٩١) وَبُرِّزَتِ الْجَحِيمُ لِلْغاوِينَ ** (91) and hell will be disclosed to the misguided, so they see it uncovered, and they regret that they are the ones driven to it. And in the difference between the two verbs (i.e., أُزْلِفَتِ and بُرِّزَتِ) there is a preference for the side of the promise (i.e., Paradise). (٩٢) وَقِيلَ لَهُمْ أَيْنَ ما كُنْتُمْ تَعْبُدُونَ (92) and they will be told, 'Where are those whom you used to worship (٩٣) مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ ** (93) besides Allah? Where are your deities whom you claimed were your intercessors? هَلْ يَنْصُرُونَكُمْ ** (Will they help you) by repelling the punishment (العَذاب) from you, أَوْ يَنْتَصِرُونَ ** (or help themselves) by repelling it from themselves, because they and their deities will enter the Fire? (٩٤) فَكُبْكِبُوا فِيها هُمْ وَالْغاوُونَ ** (94) Then they will be tumbled into it, they and the misguided, meaning, the deities and their worshippers. And al-kabkabah (الكبكبة) is the repetition of al-kabb (الكبّ) due to the repetition of its meaning, as if the one who is thrown into the Fire tumbles repeatedly, time after time, until he settles in its bottom.

In الكافي and القمّيّ, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): هُمْ ** (They) are a people who described justice (عدلاً) with their tongues, then opposed it for something else.

In القمّيّ, and in another narration: هُمْ ** (They) are Banu Umayyah, and وَالْغاوُونَ ** (the misguided) are Banu al-Abbas.

(٩٥) وَجُنُودُ إِبْلِيسَ أَجْمَعُونَ (95) And the hosts of Iblis, all together. In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): جُنُودُ إِبْلِيسَ ** (the hosts of Iblis) are his offspring (ذرّيته) from the devils (الشياطين). (٩٦) قالُوا وَهُمْ فِيها يَخْتَصِمُونَ (96) They will say, as they dispute therein: (٩٧) تَاللهِ إِنْ كُنَّا ** (97) ‘By Allah, we were (meaning: it is that we were) surely in manifest error, لَفِي ضَلالٍ مُبِينٍ (٩٨) إِذْ نُسَوِّيكُمْ بِرَبِّ الْعالَمِينَ ** (98) when we equated you with the Lord of all the worlds. Al-Qummi said: They will say to those whom they followed: "We obeyed you just as we obeyed Allah, so you became lords (أرباباً)." (٩٩) وَما أَضَلَّنا إِلَّا الْمُجْرِمُونَ (99) And none led us astray except the guilty ones.’ In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): It means the polytheists (المشركين) whom these people took as examples and followed them in their polytheism (شركهم). They are the people of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم; there is none among them from the Jews or the Christians. The confirmation of this is the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: كَذَّبَتْ قَبْلَهُمْ قَوْمُ نُوحٍ كَذَّبَ أَصْحابُ الْأَيْكَةِ كَذَّبَتْ قَوْمُ لُوطٍ ** (Before them the people of Noah denied, the inhabitants of Aykah denied, the people of Lot denied). They are not the Jews who said: عُزَيْرٌ ابْنُ اللهِ ** (Uzayr is the son of Allah), nor the Christians who said: the Messiah (الْمَسِيحُ) is the son of Allah. Allah will admit the Jews and the Christians into the Fire, and He will admit every people according to their deeds. And their saying: وَما أَضَلَّنا إِلَّا الْمُجْرِمُونَ ** (And none led us astray except the guilty ones) is because they invited us to their path. That is the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, concerning them when He gathers them into the Fire: قالَتْ أُخْراهُمْ لِأُولاهُمْ رَبَّنا هؤُلاءِ أَضَلُّونا فَآتِهِمْ عَذاباً ضِعْفاً مِنَ النَّارِ ** (The last of them will say to the first of them, ‘Our Lord! These are the ones who led us astray. So give them a double punishment of the Fire.’) And His saying: كُلَّما دَخَلَتْ أُمَّةٌ لَعَنَتْ أُخْتَها حَتَّى إِذَا ادَّارَكُوا فِيها جَمِيعاً ** (Whenever a group enters, it curses its sister group, until, when they are all gathered therein). Some of them will disavow others, and some of them will curse others. He means that some of them will argue against others, hoping for victory (الفلج) so that they might escape the enormity of what has befallen them. But it is not a time for trial (بلوى) or testing (اختبار), nor for accepting an excuse (معذرة), nor a time for salvation (نجاة).

(١٠٠) فَما لَنا مِنْ شافِعِينَ. (100) So we have no intercessors,

(١٠١) وَلا صَدِيقٍ حَمِيمٍ. (101) nor any close friend.

In al-Mahasin, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The intercessors (الشافعون) are the Imams (الأئمّة) (peace be upon them), and the friend (الصّديق) is one of the believers (المؤمنين).

And in al-Qummi, narrated from both of them (peace be upon them): By Allah, We shall certainly intercede for the sinners among our followers (شيعتنا) until our enemies say, when they see that: فَما لَنا مِنْ شافِعِينَ وَلا صَدِيقٍ حَمِيمٍ. (So we have no intercessors, nor any close friend.)

And in al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Indeed, intercession (الشفاعة) is accepted, but it is not accepted for a Nasibi (ناصب). And indeed, the believer (المؤمن) intercedes for his neighbor even if he has no good deed (حسنة). He says: "O Lord, my neighbor used to ward off harm (الأذى) from me." So he intercedes for him. Then Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, says: "I am your Lord, and I am more deserving than you to reward on your behalf." So Allah admits him to Paradise (الجنّة) even though he has no good deed (حسنة). And indeed, the lowest among the believers in terms of intercession will intercede for thirty people. It is then that the inhabitants of the Fire say: فَما لَنا مِنْ شافِعِينَ وَلا صَدِيقٍ حَمِيمٍ. (So we have no intercessors, nor any close friend.)

And in al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace): Indeed, a man in Paradise (الجنّة) will say: "What happened to my friend so-and-so?"—and his friend is in Hellfire (الجحيم). Then Allah says: "Bring his friend out for him to Paradise (الجنّة)." Then those who remain in the Fire say: فَما لَنا مِنْ شافِعِينَ وَلا صَدِيقٍ حَمِيمٍ. (So we have no intercessors, nor any close friend.)

(١٠٢) فَلَوْ أَنَّ لَنا كَرَّةً فَنَكُونَ مِنَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ** (102) If only there were a return for us, so that we might be among the faithful! al-Qummi said: [It means] among the guided (المهتدين). He said: This is because faith (الإيمان) has already become binding upon them through their affirmation (بالإقرار).

(١٠٣) إِنَّ فِي ذلِكَ لَآيَةً ** (103) There is indeed a sign in that; [It is] a proof (حجّة) and an admonition (عظة) for whoever desires to gain insight (يستبصر) through it and take heed (يعتبر). وَما كانَ أَكْثَرُهُمْ مُؤْمِنِينَ ** yet most of them were not faithful. in Him (Allah).

(١٠٤) وَإِنَّ رَبَّكَ لَهُوَ الْعَزِيزُ ** (104) Indeed your Lord is the All-mighty, The One Who is Capable (القادر) of hastening the retribution (الانتقام). الرَّحِيمُ ** the All-merciful. [He is] the All-merciful (الرَّحِيمُ) through granting respite (بالامهال) so that they, or one of their descendants (ذرّيتهم), might believe.

(١٠٥) كَذَّبَتْ قَوْمُ نُوحٍ الْمُرْسَلِينَ ** (105) The people of Noah denied the apostles. The discussion regarding their denial (تكذيبهم) has already passed.

In al-Ikmal, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He (Noah) was sent before a people who denied the prophets (الأنبياء) who were between him and Adam (peace be upon him). And that is His saying, the Almighty: **كَذَّبَتْ قَوْمُ نُوحٍ الْمُرْسَلِينَ ** meaning those who were between him and Adam (peace be upon him).

(106) إِذْ قالَ لَهُمْ أَخُوهُمْ نُوحٌ (When their brother Noah said to them:) because he was one of them أَلا تَتَّقُونَ (Will you not be wary [of Allah]?) of Allah, so that you abandon the worship of others besides Him. (107) إِنِّي لَكُمْ رَسُولٌ أَمِينٌ (I am an honest apostle for you.) well-known for trustworthiness among you. (108) فَاتَّقُوا اللهَ وَأَطِيعُونِ (So be wary of Allah and obey me) in what He has commanded you concerning monotheism (التوحيد) and obedience to Allah. (109) وَما أَسْئَلُكُمْ عَلَيْهِ (I do not ask you any reward for it.) for what I am upon, concerning the call (الدعاء) and the advice (النصح) مِنْ أَجْرٍ إِنْ أَجْرِيَ إِلَّا عَلى رَبِّ الْعالَمِينَ (My reward lies only with the Lord of all the worlds.) (110) فَاتَّقُوا اللهَ وَأَطِيعُونِ (So be wary of Allah and obey me.) He repeated it for emphasis and to draw attention to the implication of each point—his trustworthiness (أمانته) and the cutting off of his desire [for reward] (حسم طعمه)—regarding the necessity of obeying him in what he calls them to; so how much more so when both points are combined. (111) قالُوا أَنُؤْمِنُ لَكَ وَاتَّبَعَكَ الْأَرْذَلُونَ (They said, 'Shall we believe you, when the lowest of people follow you?') Al-Qummi said: The poor (الفقراء). I say: They indicated by this that their followership was not based on consideration (نظر) and insight (بصيرة), but rather due to the expectation of wealth and elevation. (112) قالَ وَما عِلْمِي بِما كانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ (He said, 'I have no knowledge of what they have been doing.) whether they did it sincerely (إخلاصاً) or out of greed for provision (طعمة). I am only responsible for the apparent consideration. (113) إِنْ حِسابُهُمْ إِلَّا عَلى رَبِّي (Their reckoning lies only with my Lord,) for He is the Knower of the inner secrets (البواطن) لَوْ تَشْعُرُونَ (if you were aware.) you would know that, but you are ignorant, so you say what you do not know. (114) وَما أَنَا بِطارِدِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ (I will not drive away the faithful.) This is a response to what their saying implied: the demand for their expulsion and the suspension of their faith upon it, since they made the followership of the poor the impediment to their own faith. (115) إِنْ أَنَا إِلَّا نَذِيرٌ مُبِينٌ (I am only a manifest warner.') It is not fitting for me to expel the poor (الفقراء) in order to attract the wealthy (الأغنياء). (116) قالُوا لَئِنْ لَمْ تَنْتَهِ يا نُوحُ (They said, 'If you do not desist, O Noah,) from what you say لَتَكُونَنَّ مِنَ الْمَرْجُومِينَ (you will surely be among the stoned.') meaning, among those who are reviled (المشتومين) or those who are struck with stones. (117) قالَ رَبِّ إِنَّ قَوْمِي كَذَّبُونِ (He said, 'My Lord! My people have denied me.) (118) فَافْتَحْ بَيْنِي وَبَيْنَهُمْ فَتْحاً (So judge decisively between me and them,) so judge between me and them وَنَجِّنِي وَمَنْ مَعِيَ مِنَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ. (and deliver me and the faithful who are with me.') (119) فَأَنْجَيْناهُ وَمَنْ مَعَهُ فِي الْفُلْكِ الْمَشْحُونِ (So We delivered him and those who were with him in the laden ark.) the filled [ark]. Al-Qummi, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): الْمَشْحُونِ (the laden) means the equipped (المجهّز) which has been completed, and nothing remains except its launching (دفعه). (120) ثُمَّ أَغْرَقْنا بَعْدُ (Then We drowned the rest afterwards.) meaning, after His deliverance of him الْباقِينَ (the rest) from his people. (121) إِنَّ فِي ذلِكَ لَآيَةً (There is indeed a sign in that;) which became widespread and successively narrated (تواترت) وَما كانَ أَكْثَرُهُمْ مُؤْمِنِينَ (but most of them were not faithful.) (122) وَإِنَّ رَبَّكَ لَهُوَ الْعَزِيزُ الرَّحِيمُ (Indeed your Lord is the All-mighty, the All-merciful.) (123) كَذَّبَتْ عادٌ ('Ad denied) the tribe of 'Ad, and it is the name of their father الْمُرْسَلِينَ (the apostles.) (124) إِذْ قالَ لَهُمْ أَخُوهُمْ هُودٌ أَلا تَتَّقُونَ. (When their brother Hud said to them, 'Will you not be wary [of Allah]?) (125) إِنِّي لَكُمْ رَسُولٌ أَمِينٌ. (I am an honest apostle for you.) (126) فَاتَّقُوا اللهَ وَأَطِيعُونِ (So be wary of Allah and obey me.) (127) وَما أَسْئَلُكُمْ عَلَيْهِ مِنْ أَجْرٍ إِنْ أَجْرِيَ إِلَّا عَلى رَبِّ الْعالَمِينَ (I do not ask you any reward for it. My reward lies only with the Lord of all the worlds.) (128) أَتَبْنُونَ بِكُلِّ رِيعٍ (Do you build a landmark on every height) on every elevated place آيَةً (for sport?) It is said: meaning a sign (علماً) for passersby, or a building that you do not need, تَعْبَثُونَ (you build it frivolously) because you are self-sufficient without it, using the stars for guidance or your dwellings for residence. In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: Indeed, every building that is built will be a burden (وبال) upon its owner on the Day of Resurrection, except that which is indispensable.

(١٢٩) وَتَتَّخِذُونَ مَصانِعَ ** (129) and you build fortresses, It is said (they are) water reservoirs (مآخِذ الماء), or lofty palaces (قصوراً مشيّدة), and fortresses (حصوناً). لَعَلَّكُمْ تَخْلُدُونَ ** hoping that you will live forever. so you make their structures firm. (١٣٠) وَإِذا بَطَشْتُمْ ** (130) When you strike, with a whip or a sword, بَطَشْتُمْ جَبَّارِينَ ** you strike as tyrants, domineering (متسلّطين), oppressive (غاشمين), without compassion (رأفة), nor intending discipline (قصد تأديب), nor considering the consequence (العاقبة). Al-Qummi said: They kill out of anger without justification (استحقاق). (١٣١) فَاتَّقُوا اللهَ ** (131) So be wary of Allah by abandoning these things, وَأَطِيعُونِ ** and obey me. in what I call you to. (١٣٢) وَاتَّقُوا الَّذِي أَمَدَّكُمْ بِما تَعْلَمُونَ ** (132) And be wary of Him who has provided you with what you know: He repeated it, connecting to it Allah's provision (أمداد الله) to them of the types of blessings (أنواع النعم) that they know, as a justification (تعليلاً) and an alert regarding the promise of the continuation of provision (دوام الامداد) (if they obey) and the threat (الوعيد) of its cessation (الانقطاع) if they abandon (obedience). (١٣٣) أَمَدَّكُمْ بِأَنْعامٍ وَبَنِينَ (133) He has provided you with cattle and sons, (١٣٤) وَجَنَّاتٍ وَعُيُونٍ (134) and gardens and springs. (١٣٥) إِنِّي أَخافُ عَلَيْكُمْ عَذابَ يَوْمٍ عَظِيمٍ. (135) Indeed, I fear for you the punishment of a tremendous day." (١٣٦) قالُوا سَواءٌ عَلَيْنا أَوَعَظْتَ أَمْ لَمْ تَكُنْ مِنَ الْواعِظِينَ ** (136) They said, "It is all the same to us whether you advise or are not one of the advisors. For we will not desist from what we are upon. (١٣٧) إِنْ هذا إِلَّا خُلُقُ الْأَوَّلِينَ ** (137) This is nothing but the custom of the ancients, meaning: This (message) which you have brought is nothing but the custom (عادة) of the ancients; they used to fabricate (يلفّقون) things like it. Or (it means): This religion (الدين) that we are upon is nothing but the custom of the ancients, and we are following their example (مقتدون). And it was recited (وقرء) with the fathah on the khā (خ) (i.e., خَلْقُ): "This (way of life) that we have come with is nothing but the lie (كذب) of the ancients," or, "Our creation (خلقنا) is nothing but their creation (خلقهم); we live and die like them, and there is no resurrection (بعث) and no reckoning (حساب)." Thus it is said.**

(١٣٨) وَما نَحْنُ بِمُعَذَّبِينَ ** (138) 'and we will not be punished.' on account of what we are upon. (١٣٩) فَكَذَّبُوهُ فَأَهْلَكْناهُمْ بريح صَرصَر إِنَّ فِي ذلِكَ لَآيَةً وَما كانَ أَكْثَرُهُمْ مُؤْمِنِينَ (139) But they denied him, so We destroyed them with a freezing wind. There is indeed a sign in that; but most of them were not faithful. (١٤٠) وَإِنَّ رَبَّكَ لَهُوَ الْعَزِيزُ الرَّحِيمُ (140) Indeed your Lord is the All-mighty, the All-merciful. (١٤١) كَذَّبَتْ ثَمُودُ الْمُرْسَلِينَ (141) Thamud denied the apostles. (١٤٢) إِذْ قالَ لَهُمْ أَخُوهُمْ صالِحٌ أَلا تَتَّقُونَ (142) When Salih, their brother, said to them, 'Will you not be wary [of Allah]? (١٤٣) إِنِّي لَكُمْ رَسُولٌ أَمِينٌ (143) 'Indeed I am a trustworthy apostle for you. (١٤٤) فَاتَّقُوا اللهَ وَأَطِيعُونِ (144) 'So be wary of Allah and obey me. (١٤٥) وَما أَسْئَلُكُمْ عَلَيْهِ مِنْ أَجْرٍ إِنْ أَجْرِيَ إِلَّا عَلى رَبِّ الْعالَمِينَ (145) 'I do not ask you any reward for it; my reward lies only with the Lord of all the worlds. (١٤٦) أَتُتْرَكُونَ فِي ما هاهُنا آمِنِينَ (146) 'Will you be left secure in what is here, (١٤٧) فِي جَنَّاتٍ وَعُيُونٍ (147) 'in gardens and springs, (١٤٨) وَزُرُوعٍ وَنَخْلٍ طَلْعُها هَضِيمٌ ** (148) 'and tillage and palms whose spathes are soft? delicate, soft, or hanging low, broken due to the abundance of the load. (١٤٩) وَتَنْحِتُونَ مِنَ الْجِبالِ بُيُوتاً فارِهِينَ ** (149) 'and carve out houses in the mountains skillfully? skillful (حاذقين). And it was read (وقرئ) by omitting the alif, meaning: insolent (بطرين). (١٥٠) فَاتَّقُوا اللهَ وَأَطِيعُونِ. (150) 'So be wary of Allah and obey me. (١٥١) وَلا تُطِيعُوا أَمْرَ الْمُسْرِفِينَ (151) 'And do not obey the command of the extravagant, (١٥٢) الَّذِينَ يُفْسِدُونَ فِي الْأَرْضِ وَلا يُصْلِحُونَ ** (152) 'those who cause corruption in the earth and do not set things right.' In this is an indication of the completeness of their corruption. (١٥٣) قالُوا إِنَّما أَنْتَ مِنَ الْمُسَحَّرِينَ ** (153) They said, 'You are only one of those who are bewitched. It was said: meaning, one of those who have been bewitched frequently until their intellect has been overcome, or one of those possessing السّحر, which is the lung (الريّة), meaning: one of the human beings (الاناسيّ). Al-Qummi says: أجوف (hollow/empty). His saying أجوف (masculine), whose feminine is جوفاء, means one possessing a belly (ذى بطن), like أعور (one-eyed) and عوراء (feminine one-eyed). Meaning: You are one possessing a belly, like the creation of people, and if you were a messenger, you would not be like us.

(١٥٤) ما أَنْتَ إِلَّا بَشَرٌ مِثْلُنا ** (You are nothing but a human being like us) An emphasis on the second meaning. فَأْتِ بِآيَةٍ إِنْ كُنْتَ مِنَ الصَّادِقِينَ ** (So bring a sign, if you are among the truthful) in your claim. (١٥٥) قالَ هذِهِ ناقَةٌ ** (He said, 'This is a she-camel) meaning, after Allah brought her forth from the rock through his supplication, just as they had proposed, as previously discussed. لَها شِرْبٌ ** (for her is a share of water) a portion of the water. **وَلَكُمْ شِرْبُ يَوْمٍ مَعْلُومٍ ** (and for you is a share of water on a known day.') So restrict yourselves to your share of water and do not crowd her during her drinking.

In al-Majma', narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), he said: The first spring (عين) that gushed forth on the earth is the one that Allah caused to burst forth for Salih, and He said: لَها شِرْبٌ وَلَكُمْ شِرْبُ يَوْمٍ مَعْلُومٍ. (for her is a share of water and for you is a share of water on a known day.')

(١٥٦) وَلا تَمَسُّوها بِسُوءٍ such as striking or hamstringing (عقر) فَيَأْخُذَكُمْ عَذابُ يَوْمٍ عَظِيمٍ ** (156) 'and do not touch her with any evil, lest the punishment of a tremendous day should seize you.' The day is tremendous due to the magnitude of what befalls therein, and this is more emphatic than magnifying the punishment itself. (١٥٧) فَعَقَرُوها ** (157) But they hamstrung her, The hamstringing (العقر) was attributed to all of them because the one who hamstrung her only did so with their consent (برضاهم), and for that reason, they were all seized فَأَصْبَحُوا نادِمِينَ ** and became regretful. over her hamstringing (عقرها) upon witnessing the punishment. (١٥٨) فَأَخَذَهُمُ الْعَذابُ ** (158) So the punishment seized them. the promised punishment.

In Nahj al-Balagha: Indeed, what unites people is consent (الرّضا) and displeasure (السّخط). Only one man hamstrung the she-camel of Thamud, yet Allah encompassed them all with the punishment when they encompassed him with consent (بالرضا). So He, glory be to Him, said: فَعَقَرُوها فَأَصْبَحُوا نادِمِينَ ** (But they hamstrung her, and became regretful.) It was not but that their land collapsed due to the sinking (بالخسفة), with a sound like the bellowing (خوار) of a heated iron rod (السكة المحماة) in soft earth (الأرض الخوارة). إِنَّ فِي ذلِكَ لَآيَةً وَما كانَ أَكْثَرُهُمْ مُؤْمِنِينَ** (Indeed, there is a sign in that, but most of them have not been believers.)

(١٥٩) وَإِنَّ رَبَّكَ لَهُوَ الْعَزِيزُ الرَّحِيمُ (159) Indeed your Lord is the All-mighty, the All-merciful. (١٦٠) كَذَّبَتْ قَوْمُ لُوطٍ الْمُرْسَلِينَ (160) The people of Lot denied the apostles. (١٦١) إِذْ قالَ لَهُمْ أَخُوهُمْ لُوطٌ أَلا تَتَّقُونَ (161) When Lot, their brother, said to them, ‘Will you not be wary [of Allah]? (١٦٢) إِنِّي لَكُمْ رَسُولٌ أَمِينٌ (162) Indeed I am a trustworthy apostle for you. (١٦٣) فَاتَّقُوا اللهَ وَأَطِيعُونِ (163) So be wary of Allah and obey me. (١٦٤) وَما أَسْئَلُكُمْ عَلَيْهِ مِنْ أَجْرٍ إِنْ أَجْرِيَ إِلَّا عَلى رَبِّ الْعالَمِينَ (164) I do not ask you any reward for it; my reward lies only with the Lord of all the worlds. (١٦٥) أَتَأْتُونَ الذُّكْرانَ مِنَ الْعالَمِينَ (165) Do you approach males from among the world’s people, (١٦٦) وَتَذَرُونَ ما خَلَقَ لَكُمْ رَبُّكُمْ لأجل استمتاعكم مِنْ أَزْواجِكُمْ بَلْ أَنْتُمْ قَوْمٌ عادُونَ متجاوزون عن حدّ الشهوة أو مفرطون في المعاصي. (166) and abandon what your Lord has created for you as your spouses? Rather, you are a transgressing lot.’ for the sake of your enjoyment. Transgressing the limit of lust, or excessive in sins. (١٦٧) قالُوا لَئِنْ لَمْ تَنْتَهِ يا لُوطُ لَتَكُونَنَّ مِنَ الْمُخْرَجِينَ من المنفيّين من بين أظهرنا. (167) They said, ‘If you do not desist, O Lot, you will surely be among the expelled.’ among those banished from our midst. (١٦٨) قالَ إِنِّي لِعَمَلِكُمْ مِنَ الْقالِينَ من المبغضين غاية البغض. (168) He said, ‘Indeed I am one of those who detest your conduct.’ among those who hate with the utmost hatred. (١٦٩) رَبِّ نَجِّنِي وَأَهْلِي مِمَّا يَعْمَلُونَ أي من شؤمه وعذابه. (169) ‘My Lord! Deliver me and my family from what they do.’ meaning: from its ill-omen and its punishment. (١٧٠) فَنَجَّيْناهُ وَأَهْلَهُ أَجْمَعِينَ أهل بيته والمتبعين له على دينه بإخراجهم من بينهم وقت حلول العذاب بهم. (170) So We delivered him and his family, all of them, his household and those who followed him in his religion, by removing them from their midst at the time the punishment descended upon them. (١٧١) إِلَّا عَجُوزاً هي امرأة لوط فِي الْغابِرِينَ مقدّرة في الباقين في العذاب. (171) except an old woman she is Lot’s wife فِي الْغابِرِينَ (among those who remained behind). Decreed to be among those remaining in the punishment. (١٧٢) ثُمَّ دَمَّرْنَا الْآخَرِينَ أهلكناهم. (172) Then We destroyed the rest. We annihilated them. (١٧٣) وَأَمْطَرْنا عَلَيْهِمْ مَطَراً حجارة فَساءَ مَطَرُ الْمُنْذَرِينَ قد سبق قصّتهم في سورة الأعراف. (173) And We rained down upon them a rain [of stones]. How evil was the rain of those who were warned! stones. Their story has already preceded in Surat al-A'raf. (١٧٤) إِنَّ فِي ذلِكَ لَآيَةً وَما كانَ أَكْثَرُهُمْ مُؤْمِنِينَ (174) There is indeed a sign in that; but most of them have not been believers. (١٧٥) وَإِنَّ رَبَّكَ لَهُوَ الْعَزِيزُ الرَّحِيمُ (175) Indeed your Lord is the All-mighty, the All-merciful. (١٧٦) كَذَّبَ أَصْحابُ الْأَيْكَةِ الْمُرْسَلِينَ الْأَيْكَةِ غيضة تنبت ناعم الشّجر. (176) The inhabitants of the Thicket denied the apostles. The Thicket (الْأَيْكَةِ): a dense grove that grows soft trees. (١٧٧) إِذْ قالَ لَهُمْ شُعَيْبٌ أَلا تَتَّقُونَ. (177) When Shu‘ayb said to them, ‘Will you not be wary [of Allah]? In al-Jawami', in the narration (الحديث): Indeed, Shu‘ayb, the brother of Midian (مدْينَ), was sent to them and to أَصْحابُ الْأَيْكَةِ. (the inhabitants of the Thicket). (١٧٨) إِنِّي لَكُمْ رَسُولٌ أَمِينٌ (178) Indeed I am a trustworthy apostle for you. (١٧٩) فَاتَّقُوا اللهَ وَأَطِيعُونِ (179) So be wary of Allah and obey me. (١٨٠) وَما أَسْئَلُكُمْ عَلَيْهِ مِنْ أَجْرٍ إِنْ أَجْرِيَ إِلَّا عَلى رَبِّ الْعالَمِينَ (180) I do not ask you any reward for it; my reward lies only with the Lord of all the worlds. (١٨١) أَوْفُوا الْكَيْلَ اتمّوه وَلا تَكُونُوا مِنَ الْمُخْسِرِينَ حقوق الناس بالتطفيف. (181) Fill the measure completely, and do not be among the defrauders, complete it. The rights of the people through short-changing. (١٨٢) وَزِنُوا بِالْقِسْطاسِ الْمُسْتَقِيمِ بالميزان السوي. (182) and weigh with the right balance, with the straight scale. (١٨٣) وَلا تَبْخَسُوا النَّاسَ أَشْياءَهُمْ ولا تنقصوا شيئاً من حقوقهم وَلا تَعْثَوْا فِي الْأَرْضِ مُفْسِدِينَ بالقتل والغارة وقطع الطريق. (183) and do not withhold from the people their goods, and do not act corruptly on the earth, spreading mischief. and do not diminish anything from their rights. Through killing, raiding, and highway robbery. (١٨٤) وَاتَّقُوا الَّذِي خَلَقَكُمْ وَالْجِبِلَّةَ الْأَوَّلِينَ وذوي الجبلّة الأوّلين يعني من تقدّمهم من الخلائق. (184) And be wary of Him who created you and the former generations.’ and those possessing the former nature (الجبلّة الأوّلين), meaning those creatures who preceded them. Al-Qummi said: The former creations (الخلق الأوّلين). (١٨٥) قالُوا إِنَّما أَنْتَ مِنَ الْمُسَحَّرِينَ (185) They said, ‘You are only one of the bewitched. (١٨٦) وَما أَنْتَ إِلَّا بَشَرٌ مِثْلُنا قيل أتوا بالواو للدلالة على أنّه جامع بين وصفين منافيين للرسالة مبالغة في تكذيبه وَإِنْ وانّه نَظُنُّكَ لَمِنَ الْكاذِبِينَ في دعواك. (186) You are nothing but a human like us. It was said (قيل): They brought the waw (الواو) to indicate that he combined two descriptions contradictory to apostleship, exaggerating in their denial of him. وَإِنْ ** (And) and that He نَظُنُّكَ لَمِنَ الْكاذِبِينَ ** (we indeed consider you to be a liar) in your claim. (١٨٧) فَأَسْقِطْ عَلَيْنا كِسَفاً مِنَ السَّماءِ قطعة منها وقرء بفتح السّين إِنْ كُنْتَ مِنَ الصَّادِقِينَ في دعوتك. (187) So make fragments of the sky fall upon us, a piece of it. And it was recited (وقرء) with the opening (فتح) of the sin (السّين). إِنْ كُنْتَ مِنَ الصَّادِقِينَ ** (if you are being truthful) in your call. (١٨٨) قالَ رَبِّي أَعْلَمُ بِما تَعْمَلُونَ وبعذابه منزل عليكم ما أوجبه في وقته المقدّر له. (188) He said, ‘My Lord knows best what you do.’ And He knows best concerning His punishment that will descend upon you, which He has made incumbent at its appointed time. (١٨٩) فَكَذَّبُوهُ فَأَخَذَهُمْ عَذابُ يَوْمِ الظُّلَّةِ. (189) But they denied him, so the punishment of the Day of the Shade seized them. Al-Qummi said: The Day of heat (حرّ) and scorching winds (سمائم). He said: It reached us—and Allah knows best—that heat afflicted them while they were in their houses, so they went out seeking relief (الروح) from the direction of the cloud in which Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, sent the punishment. When it covered them, فَأَخَذَتْهُمُ الرَّجْفَةُ فَأَصْبَحُوا فِي دارِهِمْ جاثِمِينَ ** (the earthquake seized them, and they lay lifeless in their homes). And it was said (وقيل): Allah imposed heat upon them for seven days until their rivers boiled, then a cloud shaded them, and they gathered beneath it, whereupon it rained fire upon them, and they were burned. إِنَّهُ كانَ عَذابَ يَوْمٍ عَظِيمٍ (Indeed, it was the punishment of a tremendous day).

(١٩٠) إِنَّ فِي ذلِكَ لَآيَةً وَما كانَ أَكْثَرُهُمْ مُؤْمِنِينَ (190) There is indeed a sign in that, but most of them have not been believers. (١٩١) وَإِنَّ رَبَّكَ لَهُوَ الْعَزِيزُ الرَّحِيمُ (191) Indeed your Lord is the All-mighty, the All-merciful. (١٩٢) وَإِنَّهُ لَتَنْزِيلُ رَبِّ الْعالَمِينَ (192) Indeed it is a sending down from the Lord of all the worlds. (١٩٣) نَزَلَ بِهِ الرُّوحُ الْأَمِينُ ** (193) The Trustworthy Spirit brought it down meaning Jibrāʾīl (جبرئيل), for he is Allah's trustee (أمين) over His revelation (وحي). And it was recited with the doubling (تشديد) of the zāy (الزّاي) and the accusative case (نصب) for الرّوح and الأمين. (١٩٤) عَلى قَلْبِكَ لِتَكُونَ مِنَ الْمُنْذِرِينَ. (194) upon your heart, that you may be one of the warners. In al-Kāfī and al-Baṣāʾir, narrated from al-Bāqir (peace be upon him): It is the Wilāyah (الولاية) for the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him). And al-Qummī narrated from al-Ṣādiq (peace be upon him): The Wilāyah (الولاية) that was sent down for the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) on the Day of Ghadīr (الغدير).

(١٩٥) **بِلِسانٍ عَرَبِيٍّ مُبِينٍ ** (195) in a clear Arabic language Clear in meaning.

In al-Kafi, narrated from one of the two (Imams) (peace be upon them both): He was asked about it, and He said: "It clarifies the languages (الألسن) but the languages (الألسن) do not clarify it."

And in al-Ilal, narrated from al-Sadiq, from his father (peace be upon them both), He said: Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, did not send down any Book or revelation except in Arabic (بالعربيّة). Thus, it would reach the ears of the Prophets in the languages of their people. But it would reach the ears of our Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم in Arabic (بالعربيّة). Then, when He spoke to His people with it, He spoke to them in Arabic (بالعربيّة), and it would reach their ears in their own language (بلسانهم). And no one would address the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم in any language they addressed Him with, except that it would reach His ears in Arabic (بالعربية). All of that was Gabriel (جبرئيل) translating for Him, as an honor (تشريفاً) from Allah for Him (ص).

(١٩٦) وَإِنَّهُ لَفِي زُبُرِ الْأَوَّلِينَ ** (196) And indeed it is in the scriptures of the former people. And indeed its meaning, or its mention, is in the books (كتب) of the former Prophets (الأنبياء الأوّلين). (١٩٧) أَوَلَمْ يَكُنْ لَهُمْ آيَةً ** (197) Is it not a sign for them [Is it not a sign for them] regarding the soundness (صحّة) of the Quran and the prophethood (نبوّة) of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله? And it was recited (قرء) as تكن (with a tāʾ) and آية (with a rafʿ). أَنْ يَعْلَمَهُ عُلَماءُ بَنِي إِسْرائِيلَ ** that the scholars of the Children of Israel know it? That they recognize Him by His description (نعته) mentioned in their books (كتبهم).

(١٩٨) وَلَوْ نَزَّلْناهُ عَلى بَعْضِ الْأَعْجَمِينَ (198) Had We sent it down to some non-Arab, (١٩٩) فَقَرَأَهُ عَلَيْهِمْ ما كانُوا بِهِ مُؤْمِنِينَ ** (199) and he had recited it to them, they would not have believed in it, due to the extreme of their obstinacy and their disdain (استنكافهم) from following the non-Arabs (العجم).

Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "لَوْ نَزَّلْنا " (Had We sent down) the Quran upon the non-Arabs (العجم), the Arabs would not have believed in it. Yet, it was sent down upon the Arabs, and the non-Arabs (العجم) believed in it. So, this is among the virtue (فضيلة) of the non-Arabs (العجم).

(٢٠٠) كَذلِكَ سَلَكْناهُ أدخلنا معانيه فِي قُلُوبِ الْمُجْرِمِينَ ** (200) Thus have We inserted it (in the hearts of the guilty) We inserted its meanings فِي قُلُوبِ الْمُجْرِمِينَ ** (in the hearts of the guilty), yet they did not believe in it out of obstinacy. (٢٠١) لا يُؤْمِنُونَ بِهِ حَتَّى يَرَوُا الْعَذابَ الْأَلِيمَ ** (201) They will not believe in it until they see the painful punishment. which compels them to believe. (٢٠٢) فَيَأْتِيَهُمْ بَغْتَةً وَهُمْ لا يَشْعُرُونَ ** (202) It will come upon them suddenly, while they are unaware. of its coming. (٢٠٣) فَيَقُولُوا هَلْ نَحْنُ مُنْظَرُونَ ** (203) Then they will say, ‘Shall we be granted a respite?’ out of regret (تَحَسُّراً) and sorrow (تَأَسُّفاً). (٢٠٤) أَفَبِعَذابِنا يَسْتَعْجِلُونَ ** (204) Do they seek to hasten Our punishment? when they say: فَأَمْطِرْ عَلَيْنا حِجارَةً مِنَ السَّماءِ فَأْتِنا بِما تَعِدُنا ** (then rain down stones upon us from the sky, or bring us what You promise us). Yet, their state upon the descent of the punishment is the request for respite (طَلَب النَّظْرَة*). (٢٠٥) أَفَرَأَيْتَ إِنْ مَتَّعْناهُمْ سِنِينَ (205) Have you considered if We let them enjoy themselves for some years, (٢٠٦) ثُمَّ جاءَهُمْ ما كانُوا يُوعَدُونَ (206) then there comes to them what they were promised, (٢٠٧) ما أَغْنى عَنْهُمْ ما كانُوا يُمَتَّعُونَ ** (207) what will their enjoyment avail them? Their prolonged enjoyment did not avail them in repelling the punishment or alleviating it.

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم was shown in his sleep Banu Umayyah ascending his pulpit after him, leading the people astray from the straight path (الصراط) backwards (القهقري). He awoke distressed and sorrowful. Jibril (Gabriel) descended and said: "O Messenger of Allah, why do I see you distressed and sorrowful?" He said: "O Jibril, I saw Banu Umayyah this night ascending my pulpit after me, leading the people astray from the straight path (الصراط) backwards (القهقري)." Jibril said: "By Him Who sent you with the truth as a Prophet, this is something I was not informed of." Then he ascended to the heaven. He did not delay long before descending to him with verses from the Quran to comfort him. He (Imam al-Sadiq عليه السلام) said: He revealed أَفَرَأَيْتَ إِنْ مَتَّعْناهُمْ سِنِينَ (Have you considered if We let them enjoy themselves for some years) and the subsequent verses. And He revealed to him إِنَّا أَنْزَلْناهُ ** (Indeed, We sent it down). He (Imam al-Sadiq عليه السلام) said: Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, made the Night of Decree (ليلة القدر) for His Prophet better than the thousand months during which Banu Umayyah ruled.

(208) وَما أَهْلَكْنا مِنْ قَرْيَةٍ إِلَّا لَها مُنْذِرُونَ ** (208) We have not destroyed any town but it had warners, [They are those] who warned its people, making the proof (الحجّة) binding. (209) ذِكْرى ** A reminder (تذكرة) وَما كُنَّا ظالِمِينَ ** (209) as a reminder, and We have not been unjust. So that We would destroy [them] before the warning. (210) وَما تَنَزَّلَتْ بِهِ الشَّياطِينُ ** (210) The devils have not brought it down, As the polytheists claimed that it is of the type of what the devils cast upon the soothsayers (الكهنة). (211) وَما يَنْبَغِي لَهُمْ ** And it is not sound (يصحّ) for them that they should bring it down وَما يَسْتَطِيعُونَ ** (211) nor is it proper for them, nor can they do it. And they are not able (يقدرون). (212) إِنَّهُمْ عَنِ السَّمْعِ ** Of the speech of the angels لَمَعْزُولُونَ ** (212) Indeed, they are kept out of hearing. Meaning, they are turned away (مصروفون) from hearing the Quran from the heaven. A barrier has been placed between them and hearing by means of the angels and the shooting stars (الشهب). It is said that this is because [hearing] is conditioned upon sharing in the purity of the essence (صفاء الذات) and accepting the emanation of the Truth (فيضان الحقّ), while their souls are wicked (خبيثة), dark (ظلمانيّة), and evil (شريرة).

(٢١٣) فَلا تَدْعُ مَعَ اللهِ إِلهاً آخَرَ فَتَكُونَ مِنَ الْمُعَذَّبِينَ ** (So do not invoke another god besides Allah, or you will be among the tormented.)
