This is of the category (من قبيل) "I mean you, but listen, O neighbor" (ايّاك أعني واسمعي يا جاره), for He (the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم) was purified (منزّهاً) from associating partners with Allah for the blink of an eye.

**(٢١٤) وَأَنْذِرْ عَشِيرَتَكَ الْأَقْرَبِينَ ** (And warn your nearest kinsfolk,) For attending to their affair is more important.

In al-Uyun and al-Majalis, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him): وَأَنْذِرْ عَشِيرَتَكَ الْأَقْرَبِينَ ** (And warn your nearest kinsfolk,) and your sincere clan (ورهطك المخلصين). He said: This is how it appears in the recitation (قراءة) of Ubayy ibn Ka’b, and it is confirmed in the Mushaf of Abdullah ibn Mas’ud. He said: And this is a high station (منزلة رفيعة), a great merit (فضل عظيم), and an elevated honor (شرف عال), when Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, meant the Ahl al-Bayt (الآل) by this, and mentioned it to the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله.

And in al-Majma': He attributed the recitation (القراءة) to al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) and Ibn Mas’ud.

And al-Qummi said: It was revealed concerning your clan (رهطك), among them the sincere ones (المخلصين). He said: It was revealed in Mecca. So the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله gathered the Banu Hashim, and they were forty men, each one of them capable of eating a young camel (الجذع) and drinking a waterskin (القربة). So He prepared for them a small amount of food (طعاماً يسيراً) according to what was possible, and they ate until they were satisfied. Then the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: "Who among you will be my successor (وصيّي), my minister (وزيري), and my vicegerent (خليفتي)?" Abu Lahab immediately (جزماً) said: "Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله has bewitched you!" So they dispersed. When the second day came, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله commanded that the same be done for them, then He gave them milk to drink until they were quenched. Then the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: "Which of you will be my successor, my minister, and my vicegerent?" Abu Lahab immediately said: "Muhammad has bewitched you!" So they dispersed. When the third day came, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله commanded that the same be done for them, then He gave them milk to drink. Then the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said to them: "Which of you will be my successor, my minister, fulfill my promises (ينجز عداتي), and pay off my debt (يقضي ديني)?" So Ali stood up, and he was the youngest of them in age (أصغرهم سنّاً), the thinnest of them in the leg (اخمشهم ساقاً), and the least of them in wealth (اقلّهم مالاً). He said: "I am, O Messenger of Allah." The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: "It is you."

And in al-Majma', through the path of the general public (طريق العامّة), there is something similar to this, and it added at the end: So the people stood up, saying to Abu Talib: "Obey your son, for he has been made a commander over you." And He mentioned it (أورده) in al-Ilal in brief, along with this addition.

And al-Qummi (said) regarding His saying: "and your clan, among them the sincere ones (ورهطك منهم المخلصون)," He said: Ali ibn Abi Talib, and Hamzah, and Ja’far, and al-Hasan, and al-Husayn, and the Imams from the Family of Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon them).

(215) **وَاخْفِضْ جَناحَكَ لِمَنِ اتَّبَعَكَ مِنَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ** (And lower your wing to the faithful who follow you.) Soften your manner towards them; this is borrowed metaphorically from a bird lowering its wing when it intends to descend.

In Misbah al-Shari'ah (مصباح الشريعة), Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) said: Allah has commanded the most honored of His creation and the master of His creatures, Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, to be humble (بالتواضع), so He said: وَاخْفِضْ جَناحَكَ لِمَنِ اتَّبَعَكَ مِنَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ ** (And lower your wing to the faithful who follow you.) And humility (التواضع) is the cultivated field (مزرعة) of reverence (الخشوع), awe (الخشية), and modesty (الحياء). And indeed, they (these qualities) are not manifested except from it and within it, and the complete, true honor (الشرف التام الحقيقي) is not preserved except for the one who is humble (المتواضع) for the sake of Allah (في ذات الله).

(216) فَإِنْ عَصَوْكَ فَقُلْ إِنِّي بَرِيءٌ مِمَّا تَعْمَلُونَ ** (216) But if they disobey you, say, 'I am absolved of what you do.' Al-Qummi said: فَإِنْ عَصَوْكَ (But if they disobey you) means after you, regarding the wilayah (ولاية) of Ali (peace be upon him) and the Imams (peace be upon them). He said: And the disobedience of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم while he is dead is like disobeying him while he is alive.

(217) وَتَوَكَّلْ عَلَى الْعَزِيزِ الرَّحِيمِ ** (217) And rely on the All-mighty, the All-merciful, Who is able to subdue His enemies and grant victory to His allies; He will suffice you against the evil of whoever disobeys you. And it was recited (وقرئ): فتوكّل (So rely).

(218) الَّذِي يَراكَ حِينَ تَقُومُ (218) Who sees you when you stand up, (219) وَتَقَلُّبَكَ فِي السَّاجِدِينَ. (219) and your movements among those who prostrate.

Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) that He said regarding الَّذِي يَراكَ حِينَ تَقُومُ ** (Who sees you when you stand up): [It refers] to prophethood (النبوّة). And regarding وَتَقَلُّبَكَ فِي السَّاجِدِينَ ** (and your movements among those who prostrate): He said: [It refers] to the loins (أصلاب) of the Prophets.

And in al-Majma', narrated from both of them (peace be upon them): They said: [It refers] to the loins (أصلاب) of the Prophets, Prophet after Prophet, until He brought him forth from the loins (صلب) of his father through marriage (نكاح), not fornication (سفاح), from the time of Adam (peace be upon him).

And from al-Baqir (peace be upon him), he said that the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله said: "Do not raise [your heads] before me, nor lower [them] before me, for I see you from behind me just as I see you from in front of me." Then he recited this Ayah.

I say: He means your heads during the prayer (الصلاة).

(٢١٩) إِنَّهُ هُوَ السَّمِيعُ الْعَلِيمُ (219) Indeed He is the All-hearing, the All-knowing. (٢٢٠) هَلْ أُنَبِّئُكُمْ عَلى مَنْ تَنَزَّلُ الشَّياطِينُ ** (220) Shall I inform you on whom the devils descend? When He clarified that it is not correct for the Qur’an to be among that which the devils descended with, He confirmed this by clarifying upon whom they do descend. (٢٢١) تَنَزَّلُ عَلى كُلِّ أَفَّاكٍ أَثِيمٍ ** (221) They descend on every habitual liar, sinner. A great liar (كذّاب), severe in sin (شديد الإثم). (٢٢٢) يُلْقُونَ السَّمْعَ وَأَكْثَرُهُمْ كاذِبُونَ ** (222) They cast [their] hearing, and most of them are liars. Meaning, the habitual liars (الافّاكون) يُلْقُونَ السَّمْعَ ** (cast their hearing) towards the devils, so they receive from them conjectures (ظنوناً) and signs (أمارات) due to the deficiency of their knowledge. Then they add to these, according to their own imaginings (تخيّلاتهم), things, most of which do not correspond [to reality].

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): There is no day or night except that all the Jinn (الجنّ) and the devils (الشياطين) visit the Imams of misguidance (أئمّة الضلال), and the Imams of guidance (أئمّة الهدى) are visited by their number of angels. Until the Night of Decree (ليلة القدر) arrives, and in it descends from the angels to the Guardian of the Affair (وليّ الأمر) of Allah’s creation—or he said: Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, appoints (قيّض) a number of devils equal to their number—then they visit the guardian of misguidance (وليّ الضلالة) and bring him falsehood (الإفك) and lies (الكذب), such that perhaps he wakes up and says, "I saw such-and-such." If the Guardian of the Affair (ولّى الامر) were asked about that, He would say, "You saw a devil who informed you of such-and-such," until He explains the interpretation (تفسيراً) to him and informs him of the misguidance (الضّلالة) upon which he is.

And in al-Khisal, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Regarding this Ayah, he said: "They are seven: المغيرة and بنان and صايد and حمزة بن عمارة البربريّ and الحارث الشّاميّ and عبد الله بن الحارث and ابو الخطّاب."

(٢٢٣) يُلْقُونَ السَّمْعَ وَأَكْثَرُهُمْ كاذِبُونَ (They cast their hearing, and most of them are liars.) That is, the fabricators (الافّاكون). They cast their hearing to the devils (الشياطين), and they receive from them conjectures (ظنوناً) and indications (أمارات) due to the deficiency of their knowledge, then they add things to them according to their imaginations, most of which does not correspond (to reality).

(٢٢٤) وَالشُّعَراءُ يَتَّبِعُهُمُ الْغاوُونَ (224) (As for) the poets, the misguided follow them.

And it was recited with takhfif (التّخفيف). It was said that this is a new commencement (استيناف) by which His being a poet is negated, as the polytheists claimed. Meaning that the followers of Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم are not misguided (غاوين), so how could He be a poet?

And al-Qummi said: It was revealed concerning those who altered the religion of Allah and violated the command of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic (عزّ وجلّ). "Have you ever seen a poet whom anyone follows? Rather, what is meant by this are those who established a religion based on their opinions (بارائهم), so the people follow them in that."

And in al-Ma'ani, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah, he said: "Have you seen a poet whom anyone follows? They are merely a people who acquired understanding (تفقّهوا) for other than Allah, so they went astray and led others astray."

And in al-Majma', narrated from al-'Ayyashi, from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "They are a people who learned and acquired understanding (تفقّهوا) without knowledge, so they went astray and led others astray."

And in al-I'tiqadat, narrated from him (peace be upon him): He was asked about this Ayah, and He replied: "They are the storytellers (القصّاص)."

(٢٢٥) أَلَمْ تَرَ أَنَّهُمْ فِي كُلِّ وادٍ يَهِيمُونَ ** (Do you not see that they wander bewildered in every valley?) It was said: And that is because most of the words of the poets are imaginations (خيالات) that have no reality (حقيقة) for them. And al-Qummi said: He means that they debate with falsehoods (الأباطيل) and argue with misleading proofs (الحجج المضلّين), and they go into every doctrine (مذهب). He means by them those who alter the religion of Allah.

(٢٢٦) وَأَنَّهُمْ يَقُولُونَ ما لا يَفْعَلُونَ ** (226) And that they say what they do not do He said: They admonish the people but are not admonished themselves, and they forbid evil (المنكر) but do not desist from it, and they enjoin good (المعروف) but do not practice it. He said: And they are those who usurped the right (حقّهم) of the Family of Muhammad (may the prayers of Allah be upon them and their family). (٢٢٧) إِلَّا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحاتِ وَذَكَرُوا اللهَ كَثِيراً وَانْتَصَرُوا مِنْ بَعْدِ ما ظُلِمُوا ** (227) except those who have faith and do righteous deeds, and remember Allah greatly, and vindicate themselves after they have been wronged. It was said that this is an exception for the believing, righteous poets who frequently remember Allah, and the majority of whose poetry is about the Oneness of Allah (التوحيد), praise of Allah the Almighty, and encouragement towards obedience to Him. And if they composed satire (هجواً), they intended thereby to vindicate themselves against the disbelievers (الكفّار) who satirized them, and to retaliate against those who satirized the Muslims, such as حسّان بن ثابت, كعب بن مالك, and كعب بن زبير.

And al-Qummi said: Then He mentioned the Family of Muhammad (may the prayers of Allah be upon them and their family) and their guided followers (شيعتهم المهتدين), so He said: إِلَّا الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا (except those who have faith)... the Ayah.

I say: It is possible to reconcile the two interpretations by intending both meanings, for the arguments of the falsifiers (المبطلين) among the people of disputation (أهل الجدل) are also mostly poetic fantasies (خيالات شعريّة) that have no reality, and deceptions (تمويهات) that are worthless, just like the sayings of the poets. Both groups are alike in that they wander in every valley, and that they say what they do not do. However, the mention of the followers of the misguided (الغاوين) is only with respect to those who hold leadership in misguidance (الإضلال) among the adherents of false schools of thought (المذاهب الباطلة). And the denial of one of the meanings in the narration (الحديث) refers back to denying exclusivity (الحصر) to it. Furthermore, the blameworthy poetry (الشعر المذموم) is not meant to be the metrical speech (الكلام المنظوم) by virtue of its meter, for how can it be, when indeed some poetry is wisdom—meaning from the metrical speech—and some of it is admonition, and some of it is praise of Allah and His vicegerents (أوليائه). Rather, [it is blameworthy] by virtue of illicit love poetry (التّشبيب بالحرام), tearing apart reputations (تمزيق الاعراض), praising those who do not deserve it, and similar things.

In al-Uyun, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), he said: "Whoever composes a verse of poetry concerning us, Allah builds a house for him in Paradise." And he said: "No one composes poetry concerning us unless he is supported by the Holy Spirit (روح القدس)."

In al-Majma', narrated from كعب بن مالك, he said: "O Messenger of Allah, what do You say about the poets?" He said: "Indeed, the believer strives (مجاهد) with his sword, and by Him in Whose hand is my soul, it is as if they are pelting them with arrows (النّبل)." He said: And the Prophet (may Allah's prayers be upon him and his family) said to حسّان بن ثابت: "Satirize them (اهجهم) or attack them (هاجهم), and the Holy Spirit (روح القدس) is with you."

In al-Jawami': He said to كعب بن مالك: "Satirize them, for by Him in Whose hand is my soul, it is more severe upon them than arrows (النبل)."

In al-Kitab al-Kashshi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "O company of the Shi'ah (يا معشر الشّيعة), teach your children the poetry of al-ʿAbdi (العبدي), for it is upon the religion of Allah."

In al-Ma'ani, narrated from him (peace be upon him): He was asked about this Ayah, "What is this great remembrance (الذكر الكثير)?" He said: "Whoever glorifies [Allah] with the glorification (تسبيح) of Fatimah al-Zahra (peace be upon her) has indeed remembered Allah greatly."

In al-Kafi, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): "Whoever remembers Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, in secret has remembered Allah greatly. Indeed, the hypocrites (المنافقين) used to remember Allah openly but did not remember Him in secret. So Allah the Almighty said: يُراؤُنَ النَّاسَ وَلا يَذْكُرُونَ اللهَ إِلَّا قَلِيلاً (They show off to people and do not remember Allah except a little.) وَسَيَعْلَمُ الَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا أَيَّ مُنْقَلَبٍ يَنْقَلِبُونَ (And soon the wrongdoers will know what fate they will meet.)"

Al-Qummi said: Then He mentioned their enemies and those who wronged them, so His mention is majestic, He said: وَسَيَعْلَمُ الَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا (And soon the wrongdoers will know)—[meaning] those who wronged the Family of Muhammad (آلَ مُحَمَّدٍ) regarding their right (حَقَّهُمْ)—**أَيَّ مُنْقَلَبٍ يَنْقَلِبُونَ ** (what fate they will meet). By Allah, thus was it revealed.

In al-Jawami': This recitation (القراءة) is attributed to Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him).

In Thawab al-A'mal and al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "Whoever recites the three Tawasin Surahs (سور الطّواسين الثّلاث) on the night of Friday will be among the vicegerents (أولياء الله) of Allah, in His proximity and under His protection. Misery will never afflict him in this world, and in the Hereafter, He will be given from Paradise until he is satisfied and beyond his satisfaction, and Allah will marry him to one hundred wives from the wide-eyed Houris (الحور العين)."

And it is added in al-Majma': "And Allah will settle him in the Garden of Eden (جنّة عدن), in the middle of Paradise, along with the Prophets (النبيّين), the Messengers (المرسلين), and the rightly guided Successors (الوصيّين الرّاشدين)."
